CXMT Corporation (长鑫科技集团股份有限公司) — Prospectus

Statement to Investors

I. Purpose of the Issuer's Listing

(I) Enhancing the Company's R&D and Innovation Capabilities, Accelerating Technology Iteration and Improvement of Product Portfolio

Since its establishment, the Company has consistently focused on the research and development, design, production, and sales of DRAM products. The DRAM industry undergoes rapid technology iteration; currently, products such as DDR5 and LPDDR5/5X are accelerating their penetration into downstream markets. First-mover advantages in technology and products, sustained R&D investment, and efficient technology iteration capabilities are crucial for the Company to build its market competitiveness. At the same time, the integrated circuit industry is a typical talent-intensive industry, and attracting and retaining top-tier talent is a prerequisite for the sustained development of both the industry and the Company. This offering and listing will, on the one hand, effectively enhance the Company's capacity for continuous R&D investment through the proceeds raised, and on the other hand, will elevate the Company's brand influence, attract top global talent to join the Company, and strengthen the technological innovation capabilities of the Company's R&D team.

(II) Accelerating the Company's Capacity Construction and Upgrade, Enhancing the Company's Market Competitiveness and Profitability

The Company's production capacity has ranked first in China and fourth globally; however, there remains a certain gap compared to the top three international leading DRAM manufacturers, and the Company's production capacity is also far below the enormous domestic market demand. With the steady advancement of construction projects funded by the proceeds from this listing, the Company will accelerate process upgrades, thereby achieving lower unit costs, stronger market competitiveness and profitability, more effectively meeting the robust future demand of global downstream markets, and helping the Company secure a more advantageous position in the global DRAM market.

(III) Capitalizing on Broad Market Growth Opportunities, Accelerating Market Expansion and Promoting Industrial Development

The integrated circuit industry is a strategic, foundational, and pioneering industry supporting economic and social development, and is a key force driving the new round of technological revolution and industrial transformation. As a strategically foundational product in the development of the integrated circuit industry, DRAM is one of the core chips of the digital economy era and an important cornerstone of national information infrastructure strategic security. China is one of the world's major DRAM consumption markets; however, the domestic self-sufficiency rate of locally branded DRAM products remains relatively low, with broad future development potential.

After years of development, the Company has broken through key core DRAM technologies and successfully achieved independent R&D, design, and commercial mass production of its products, filling the long-standing gap of mainland Chinese DRAM products in the global market. This offering and listing will effectively accelerate the Company's capacity scale construction, process technology upgrades, product portfolio improvement, and product performance optimization, enhancing the Company's ability to ensure supply to meet enormous market demand, and assisting China's information infrastructure construction and the development of new productive forces,

1-1-2

contributing to safeguarding national information and industrial security and building a modern national industrial system.

As the leading enterprise in China's DRAM industry, this offering and listing will not only promote the Company's own high-quality development, but will also effectively drive the coordinated development of enterprises at key links in the industrial chain, including "memory chip design companies, EDA vendors, semiconductor material manufacturers, semiconductor equipment and component manufacturers, module manufacturers, and downstream terminal application manufacturers," further enhancing the overall strength of China's integrated circuit industrial chain and propelling China's integrated circuit industry toward a higher level.

(IV) Improving Corporate Governance and Continuously Enhancing Core Competitiveness, Creating Long-Term Value for Shareholders

During the reporting period, the Company achieved sustained growth in operating revenue and a rapid narrowing of net losses, driven by its continuously optimized product structure, iteratively advancing process capabilities, increasingly mature operational capabilities, and broad market recognition. Upon completion of this offering and listing, the Company will leverage the listing platform and a more complete governance structure to further capitalize on its advantages in technology, products, market, and organizational management, continuously enhance its core competitiveness, and create long-term value returns for the Company's shareholders.

II. Status of Establishing and Improving the Issuer's Modern Enterprise System

Since its inception, the Company has consistently committed to establishing and improving a modern enterprise system. Since converting to a joint stock company, the Company has established a governance structure consisting of the General Meeting of Shareholders, the Board of Directors (including independent directors), various special committees, and the management team. Relevant rules of procedure have been formulated, specifying the responsibilities and authorities of independent directors and the Board Secretary, forming a modern enterprise system that satisfies the requirements of laws, regulations, and normative documents including the Company Law, the Securities Law, and the Listing Rules. At the same time, with a focus on long-term sustainable development, the Company has formulated a clear profit distribution plan and shareholder return scheme, and has taken concrete measures to protect the interests of the Company and its minority shareholders.

III. Necessity of the Issuer's Current Financing and Plan for Use of Proceeds

The use of proceeds from this offering will revolve around the Company's core business, and will be primarily used for the Technology Upgrade and Renovation Project for Memory Wafer Manufacturing Mass Production Lines, the DRAM Memory Technology Upgrade Project, and the Dynamic Random Access Memory Forward-Looking Technology Research and Development Project. These represent expansions and extensions of the Company's existing business based on the characteristics of its current development stage and the requirements of technological R&D innovation. They are both an inevitable requirement for the Company to further enhance its technological and R&D strength, and an important measure for the Company to continuously deepen its process technology development and accumulation, continuously explore frontier technologies, and enhance its core competitiveness.

1-1-3

IV. The Issuer's Ability to Continue as a Going Concern and Future Development Plans

Since its establishment, the Company has consistently devoted itself to the R&D, design, production, and sales of DRAM products, and has now developed into the largest, most technologically advanced, and most comprehensively positioned integrated DRAM R&D, design, and manufacturing enterprise in China. The Company has achieved certain economies of scale and continues to conduct technology iteration and capacity construction, while maintaining continuous technological leadership through ongoing independent innovation and R&D. The Company's business development prospects are promising, its financial health is sound, and it possesses strong going-concern capabilities.

The Company will uphold its mission of "empowering the information society with memory technology to improve human life," adhere to high-level technological R&D, continuously accumulate and advance process and product technologies, consolidate its core technology foundations, accelerate capacity deployment and construction, expand a diversified product portfolio, continuously strengthen its talent pipeline and operational management systems, and ultimately realize its development vision of "becoming a technologically leading and commercially successful semiconductor memory company."

Chairman's Signature:

Zhu Yiming (朱一明)

Term Refers To
Byte Byte, a data measurement unit in computer technology; 1 Byte = 8 bits; commonly used to denote memory capacity
Gb, GB, ZB Units of computer storage capacity: Gb refers to Gigabit; GB refers to Giga Byte; ZB refers to Zetta Byte; 1 GB = 8 Gb; 1 ZB = 1,0244 GB
MGB MGB refers to Million GB; 1 MGB = 106 GB
Mbps A unit measuring data transfer capacity; 1 Mbps represents the transmission of 1 megabit (i.e., 1,000,000 bits) per second
Die A single chip with complex circuit functions produced by a wafer foundry through tape-out; further formed into a chip product after packaging and testing. Commonly referred to as a "bare die" during the production process
ECC Error Checking and Correction, or Error Correction Coding; an algorithm used to detect and correct errors that occur during data transmission or storage
DIMM Dual Inline Memory Module; one of the mainstream form factors for DRAM memory modules, commonly known as a "memory stick"
RDIMM Registered DIMM; a memory module that uses a specific chip to buffer address, command, and control signals; primarily used in servers
UDIMM Unbuffered DIMM; a memory module in which address and control signals are not passed through a buffer and require no timing adjustment; primarily used in desktop computers
SODIMM Small Outline DIMM; a compact dual inline memory module primarily used in laptop computers
MRDIMM Multiplexed Rank DIMM; a higher-bandwidth server memory module
LPCAMM Low Power Compression Attached Memory Module; a new memory module standard that combines the low-power characteristics of LPDDR memory with the compact design of the CAMM module (a new memory module standard using LPDDR5X memory chips, which achieves a thinner and lighter form factor, higher capacity, lower power consumption, and improved thermal performance through optimized module layout and a more compact connector); designed to provide high-performance, low-power, and upgradeable memory solutions for thin-and-light laptops, mobile workstations, and similar devices
CXL Compute Express Link; an open interconnect protocol standard introduced in 2019, designed to provide an efficient, high-speed, low-latency interface between CPUs and dedicated accelerators and high-performance storage systems, in order to meet the needs of resource sharing, memory pooling, and efficient computing scheduling
JEDEC Joint Electron Device Engineering Council; the JEDEC Solid State Technology Association, founded in 1958; the authoritative standardization body for the global microelectronics industry, responsible for formulating global industry standards and specifications for DRAM products
IDM Integrated Device Manufacturer; a vertically integrated manufacturer that integrates chip design, chip manufacturing, packaging and testing, and product sales; a type of business model in the semiconductor industry
Fabless A fabless chip design company that engages only in chip design and sales, outsourcing wafer fabrication, packaging, and testing to specialized vendors
Bank Group A memory bank group; a fundamental structural unit within a chip used to organize and manage memory cells; commonly used in DRAM chips to improve memory efficiency and access speed
Row Hammer A type of security vulnerability that relies on charge leakage between adjacent memory cells, enabling a threat actor to flip 1s and 0s and alter the contents of memory; this phenomenon is known as Bit Flips and can be exploited to gain elevated privileges
RDL Redistribution Layer; a technology that changes the location of I/O contact points through wafer-level metal wiring processes and bump processes to achieve circuit redistribution; widely used in advanced semiconductor packaging
DVFS Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling; a technique that dynamically adjusts a chip's operating frequency and voltage based on the varying computational demands of the applications being run (for the same chip, a higher frequency requires a higher voltage), thereby achieving energy savings
DFT Design For Test; a design-for-testability approach in chip design whereby, without compromising functionality, special test structures are incorporated during the chip design process so that after manufacturing, tests can be conducted to identify defects that may have occurred during fabrication or packaging and that prevent the chip from functioning properly

Data presented in this Prospectus may differ slightly in the last digit from results calculated based on the individual items set forth herein, due to rounding.

Section 2: Overview

This Overview is intended only as a brief summary of the full text of the Prospectus. Investors should carefully read the full text of the Prospectus before making any investment decision.

I. Notice of Material Matters

(I) Special Risk Warnings

The Company draws investors' particular attention to the following risks under "Risk Factors" and urges investors to carefully read the entire content of "Section 3: Risk Factors" of this Prospectus.

1. The DRAM industry is characterized by high capital intensity and high technical barriers. During the Reporting Period, the Company was in a stage of rapid capacity construction and ramp-up, while continuing to increase R&D investment. The Company has not yet achieved profitability during the Reporting Period and has accumulated unrecovered losses.

For each period of the Reporting Period, the net profit attributable to the owners of the parent company was RMB -8,328,003,900, RMB -16,339,777,200, RMB -7,144,887,200, and RMB -2,332,058,200, respectively. As of June 30, 2025, the Company's accumulated losses amounted to RMB -40,857,338,700. The Company has not yet achieved profitability and has accumulated unrecovered losses.

The Company has not yet achieved profitability during the Reporting Period primarily because: (1) The DRAM industry has a scale-oriented nature. In order to enhance market share and competitiveness, the Company needs to continuously increase production capacity, which requires substantial fixed asset investment in facilities and production lines, resulting in significant depreciation of fixed assets. During the Reporting Period, the Company was in a stage of rapid capacity ramp-up, with production lines continuously being built and upgraded; after the relevant assets were transferred to fixed assets, the resulting fixed costs such as depreciation were high and continued to increase. Although the Company's EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization) turned positive in 2024, the economies of scale from newly added capacity had not yet been fully realized, and the revenue base was still insufficient to cover all costs and expenses; (2) The DRAM industry has extremely high technical barriers. The Company has continuously engaged in R&D iteration of products and process technologies in order to catch up with international advanced levels as quickly as possible, improve product competitiveness, and meet market demand, resulting in continued increases in R&D expenditure during the Reporting Period.

In the coming years, the depreciation and amortization, and R&D expenditure arising from the Company's continued capacity expansion and R&D investment will continue to exert pressure on the Company's profits in the short term. If the Company's product R&D and commercialization fall short of expectations, or the release of economies of scale falls short of expectations, the Company may continue to remain unprofitable with accumulated unrecovered losses, or the losses may even further expand, which would have a certain adverse impact on the Company's profitability and result in the inability to pay cash dividends to shareholders in the short term.

2. Risk of fluctuations in the Company's product selling prices and operating performance due to macroeconomic volatility and industry cyclicality

The DRAM industry is significantly affected by fluctuations in market supply and demand and is characterized by cyclicality. In addition, macroeconomic volatility and the capacity control strategies of major manufacturers under a highly concentrated industry structure further exacerbate industry cyclical volatility. From 2022 to 2023, affected by high upstream DRAM product inventory levels and sluggish downstream market demand, the DRAM industry went through a deep downturn cycle, with DRAM product selling prices declining sharply. It was not until the second half of 2023 that the market began to gradually recover and product prices gradually stabilized and rebounded. During this industry cycle, companies within the industry generally incurred losses.

During the Reporting Period, on one hand, the selling prices of the Company's major DRAM products fluctuated significantly and were greatly influenced by industry cycles. In 2023 and 2024, the year-on-year changes in the selling prices of the Company's major DRAM products were -43.54% and +55.08%, respectively. In 2023, although the Company achieved significant growth in production and sales volume, the decline in unit prices significantly impacted the Company's revenue growth rate. On the other hand, the decline in unit prices led to increased provisions for inventory write-downs, which impacted the Company's profits. In 2023, the increase in inventory write-down losses was the primary reason for the year-on-year expansion of the Company's losses.

In the future, if macroeconomic conditions deteriorate adversely, downstream market demand in the DRAM industry weakens, and DRAM product supply significantly exceeds demand, the Company's product selling prices and operating performance will be adversely affected.

3. Risk of large fixed asset construction investment and depreciation charges

At the end of each period of the Reporting Period, the carrying values of the Company's fixed assets were RMB 68,015,605,700, RMB 84,452,072,900, RMB 153,132,016,000, and RMB 171,593,342,300, respectively, accounting for 46.05%, 43.81%, 56.38%, and 59.19% of total assets at the respective period ends — a high proportion that has continued to increase. Depreciation of fixed assets charged during each period of the Reporting Period amounted to RMB 5,242,713,300, RMB 10,555,252,600, RMB 14,875,435,800, and RMB 11,349,488,600, respectively, showing an upward trend. The Company continues to carry out capacity construction; the carrying value of fixed assets is expected to increase further, and the resulting significant depreciation will have a certain impact on the Company's performance over the depreciation period. At the same time, the Company's fixed assets may be subject to the risk of impairment due to damage, technological upgrades and iterations, or changes in technology roadmap, which would adversely affect the Company's operating performance.

4. Risk of inventory write-down

During the Reporting Period, in line with steady growth in sales scale, the balances of the Company's raw materials, work-in-progress, and finished goods inventories at the end of each period also showed an upward trend. Inventory balances at the end of each period were RMB 9,433,769,200, RMB 14,180,475,000, RMB 21,214,508,700, and RMB 28,072,588,700, respectively. Inventory write-down provisions at the end of each period were RMB 2,517,033,100, RMB 3,444,297,700, RMB 2,664,979,100, and RMB 1,986,170,200, respectively. The integrated circuit industry has cyclical characteristics; macroeconomic conditions, industry cycles, and capacity cycles all have significant impacts on the industry. During each period of the Reporting Period, the Company's inventory write-down losses were RMB -4,078,101,800, RMB -11,500,210,600, RMB -1,826,884,100, and RMB -627,837,300, respectively. These were significantly impacted by the DRAM industry cycle, particularly in 2023 when the industry downturn led to a sharp increase in inventory write-down provisions. In the future, if industry cycles, market demand, or market prices for the Company's products change such that the selling prices of certain inventories cannot cover their costs, the Company will face the risk of increased inventory write-downs, which would adversely affect the Company's asset values and profitability.

5. Risk of supply chain instability due to international trade frictions

After years of development, the semiconductor supply chain has formed a highly specialized and efficiently collaborative global division of labor. However, current geopolitical conflicts are accelerating the restructuring of the supply chain. Although the Company has consistently adhered to market-oriented operations since its founding and has voluntarily complied with relevant international import and export rules and regulations, if relevant countries further strengthen restrictive policies in the short term, the Company may face the risk of supply chain instability, which would have a certain adverse impact on the Company's production and business operations.

6. Risk that product and technology R&D may fall short of expectations

The DRAM industry is a technology-intensive industry with high technical barriers, rich end-application scenarios for products, and a rapid pace of product upgrading and iteration. The DRAM industry requires a high level of technical sophistication, necessitating extensive preliminary technical validation and continuous subsequent R&D practice, resulting in long R&D cycles. Over the years, the Company has adhered to the path of independent R&D and has established its current core technology platform through R&D and innovation. If the Company's R&D direction deviates from market demand, the technical performance of new products falls behind competitors, or technology R&D and product upgrades fail to meet expectations, leading to the Company's inability to keep pace with industry frontiers and market demands in the future, it may have an adverse impact on the Company's future operations.

7. Risk of having no controlling shareholder or actual controller

Based on the Company's shareholding structure, shareholders directly holding more than 5% of the Company's shares are Qinghui Jidian (清辉集电), Changxin Integrated (长鑫集成), the National Integrated Circuit Industry Investment Fund II (大基金二期), Hefei Jixin (合肥集鑫), and Anhui Provincial Investment (安徽省投), holding 21.67%, 11.71%, 8.73%, 8.37%, and 7.91% of Chang Xin Technology's equity, respectively. There is no single shareholder holding more than 50% of shares, and the Company's shareholding structure is relatively dispersed. Among these, the Company's largest shareholder, Qinghui Jidian, is a structure with no actual controller. Based on the composition of the Company's Board of Directors, the Board consists of 11 members, including 4 independent directors. Among the 7 non-independent directors, no single shareholder can, through actual control of voting rights, determine the election of more than half of the members of Chang Xin Technology's Board of Directors. Therefore, the Company has no controlling shareholder or actual controller.

Since the Company has no controlling shareholder or actual controller, there may be a risk that disagreements among shareholders or directors could reduce decision-making efficiency, leading to missed development opportunities. At the same time, the Company's shareholding structure is relatively dispersed, and there is a possibility that control may change after listing, thereby posing potential risks to the stability of the Company's operations and development.

8. Risk that additional expenses and depreciation and amortization arising from fund-raising investment projects may affect the Company's operating performance

Pursuant to the plan for the use of proceeds, during the construction period of the projects to be funded by the proceeds of this offering, the Company will incur additional related expenditures. After the completion of the projects funded by the proceeds of this offering, the additional fixed asset investment will lead to increased depreciation and amortization charges, which will directly affect the Issuer's annual operating performance. As the construction, completion, and revenue generation of the fund-raising investment projects require a certain period of time and may be subject to various uncertainties, if major adverse changes occur in the market environment or other factors in which the Issuer operates in the future, resulting in the fund-raising investment projects failing to achieve their projected returns, the aforementioned additional depreciation and amortization and period expenses may affect the Company's profits, resulting in a risk of decline in future operating performance.

(II) Special Matters and Forward-Looking Information Regarding the Company's Failure to Achieve Profitability During the Reporting Period

During the Reporting Period, the Company's product sales volume and operating revenue grew rapidly. However, due to the high asset and R&D investment intensity, high technical barriers, and long customer certification cycles of the DRAM industry, compounded by the effects of the Company's stage of capacity construction and the volatility cycles of memory chips, the Company has not yet achieved profitability and has accumulated unrecovered losses.

As the Company steadily advances capacity construction, product R&D, and process technology improvements, the Company expects to maintain sound ongoing operational capability in the future. Based on the Company's current forward-looking information, it is projected that profitability may be achieved in 2026 or 2027. Whether the Company can achieve profitability in 2026 depends primarily on the combined effect of average unit prices and average monthly shipment volume (average monthly wafer shipments). Based on conservative projections, as previously built capacity is gradually constructed and successfully ramped up, shipment volumes are expected to grow steadily. If the Company's average product prices remain at a level slightly below the actual average prices in September 2025, the Company is expected to achieve profitability in 2026. If the Company's average unit prices and shipment volumes in 2026 fall short of expectations, profitability may not be achievable for that year.

The above projections do not constitute a profit forecast or performance commitment. The forward-looking information above is based on forecasts built on speculative assumptions and is subject to material uncertainty. Investors should exercise caution when using this information for investment decisions.

The risk of the Company's failure to achieve profitability during the Reporting Period has been drawn to investors' attention under "I. Notice of Material Matters — (I) Special Risk Warnings" in this section. The specific reasons for the Company's failure to achieve profitability, an analysis of the related impacts, and forward-looking information regarding the achievement of future profitability are set out in "Section 6: Financial and Accounting Information and Management Discussion and Analysis," Sub-section "IX. Analysis of Operating Results," Sub-section "(VIII) Circumstances of Failure to Achieve Profitability and the Existence of Accumulated Unrecovered Losses" of this Prospectus.

(III) Important Commitments Made by Relevant Parties in Connection with This Offering

The Company draws investors' attention to the important commitments made by the Company, relevant shareholders, directors, senior management, core technical personnel, the sponsor and securities service institutions of this offering, as well as the binding measures for failure to fulfill such commitments. Other Commitments Made by the Issuer and Other Responsible Parties in Connection with the Issuer's Current Offering and Listing" of this Prospectus for the specific commitment matters.

(IV) Distribution Arrangements for Retained Earnings Prior to This Offering and Dividend Distribution Policy After the Offering

As resolved by the shareholders' meeting of the Company in connection with the listing: prior to the current offering and listing, the Company may distribute profits pursuant to a shareholders' meeting resolution. The retained undistributed profits (accumulated unrecovered losses) shall be shared (borne) in full by both existing and new shareholders after the current offering and listing, in proportion to their respective shareholdings after listing.

The Company draws investors' attention to the Company's dividend distribution policy and the three-year dividend return plan after this offering and listing. For details, please refer to "Section 9: Investor Protection," Sub-section "II. Issuer's Dividend Distribution Policy" of this Prospectus.

IV. Information on the Issuer's Principal Business

(I) Principal Business, Principal Products or Services

Chang Xin Technology (长鑫科技) is China's largest, most technically advanced, and most comprehensively positioned integrated DRAM R&D, design, and manufacturing enterprise. Since its establishment in 2016, the Company has consistently focused on the R&D, design, production, and sales of DRAM products. The Company has adopted a "generation-leapfrogging R&D" strategy, completing mass production across its first through fourth generations of process technology platforms and achieving product coverage and iteration from DDR4 and LPDDR4X to DDR5 and LPDDR5/5X. The Company's core products and process technologies have now reached internationally advanced levels.

The Company actively captures industry development trends and continuously iterates its products, having formed a diversified product lineup including DDR series, LPDDR series, and others, and is able to provide diversified product solutions including DRAM wafers, DRAM chips, and DRAM modules, effectively meeting the market demands of servers, mobile devices, personal computers, intelligent vehicles, and other sectors. The Company has a total of three 12-inch DRAM wafer fabs located in Hefei and Beijing. According to Omdia data, based on capacity and shipment volume statistics, the Company has become the No. 1 DRAM manufacturer in China and the No. 4 DRAM manufacturer globally. The Company places great emphasis on independent technology R&D and innovation, having built a comprehensive and sound core technology system across all business segments including DRAM product design, manufacturing processes, packaging and testing, and module design and application. Its key core technologies have reached internationally advanced levels, giving rise to extensive proprietary intellectual property rights. The Company has accumulated a broad base of high-quality customers and, together with upstream and downstream partners, has built an industrial ecosystem of mutual dependence and shared development.

(II) Principal Raw Materials and Key Suppliers

During the Reporting Period, the principal raw materials used in the Company's production and operations include chemicals, spare parts, photoresists, silicon wafers, gases, and sputtering targets. For information on key suppliers, please refer to "Section 5: Business and Technology," Sub-section "V. Issuer's Procurement and Key Suppliers" of this Prospectus.

(III) Principal Production Model

In the wafer manufacturing segment, the Company formulates production plans based on customer orders, market forecasts, and its own capacity and process conditions. Strict process control and yield management are implemented throughout the production process, and DRAM products are stably mass-produced and rapidly delivered through coordination between front-end and back-end processes. The product production process can be divided into the following stages: formulating the production plan, preparing the work schedule, confirming work readiness and executing production, process monitoring and control, and wafer output and warehousing.

In the chip packaging and final testing segment, on the basis of independent technology R&D, the Company adopts an outsourced production model, entrusting the packaging process to outsourced packaging and testing vendors. For testing operations, the Company currently adopts a model primarily based on in-house testing supplemented by outsourced testing: a portion of testing is completed by its wholly-owned subsidiary, Changxin Products Hefei (长鑫产品合肥), while another portion is outsourced to packaging and testing vendors.

In the module processing and testing segment, the Company primarily completes operations through outsourced processes. The Company's production department raises output requirements based on the production plan, and the planning department is responsible for formulating the outsourced production schedule in accordance with the Company's relevant standards and allocating processing quantities to each outsourced vendor.

For further details, please refer to "Section 5: Business and Technology," Sub-section "I. Issuer's Principal Business, Principal Products and Services," Sub-section "(II) Issuer's Principal Operating Model" of this Prospectus.

(IV) Principal Sales Methods and Key Customers

During the Reporting Period, the Company adopted a sales model combining distribution and direct sales. The Company's distribution model is a buy-out sales model, and distribution is one of the Company's primary sales models. Distributors help the Company rapidly establish sales channels, expand market share, achieve faster turnover of products and capital, and improve the Company's operational efficiency and responsiveness. During the Reporting Period, the Company's top five customers primarily comprised well-known industry distributors and leading large manufacturers in downstream application segments, and the composition remained relatively stable. There was no instance of sales to a single customer exceeding 50% of operating revenue, nor was there excessive reliance on a small number of customers. For details, please refer to "Section 5: Business and Technology," Sub-section "IV. Issuer's Sales and Key Customers" of this Prospectus.

(V) Industry Competition and the Issuer's Competitive Position

The DRAM industry has extremely high technical and capital barriers. Leading enterprises that have already achieved a certain scale can reduce costs through economies of scale, thereby consolidating their existing advantages. Influenced by these industry characteristics, the DRAM industry has evolved from dozens of companies in the early stage of development in the 1980s to the current situation where the world's major manufacturers include Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix, Micron Technology, and Chang Xin Technology (长鑫科技).

According to Omdia data, based on sales revenue, Samsung Electronics held a 40.35% share of the global DRAM market in 2024, ranking first. SK Hynix and Micron Technology held 33.19% and 20.73% of the global DRAM market in 2024, respectively, ranking second and third. These three companies together account for more than 90% of the global DRAM market share.

In recent years, among domestic DRAM manufacturers, Chang Xin Technology is gradually entering the ranks of major players. Based on calculations using Omdia data, as measured by DRAM sales revenue in Q2 2025, Chang Xin Technology's global market share has increased to 3.97%, and is expected to grow further along with technology development and capacity construction.

In addition to the aforementioned manufacturers, other companies with a certain market share are mainly concentrated in Taiwan, China, including Nanya Technology, Winbond Electronics, and PSMC. Other semiconductor companies in Mainland China have also entered the DRAM business, but most focus on chip design.

1. Analysis of Financial Position

As of September 30, 2025, the Company's total assets increased by 11.52% compared to year-end 2024, primarily due to further increases in the scale of the Company's fixed assets and inventories as the Company's capacity continued to be constructed and expanded. Total liabilities remained broadly stable, and total equity increased by 24.76% compared to year-end 2024 in line with the growth in total assets.

2. Analysis of Operating Results

For January–September 2025, driven by the Company's continued growth in production and sales scale, continuous optimization of product mix, and the rapid increase in DRAM product prices since the second half of 2025, the Company's operating revenue grew rapidly, increasing by 97.79% year-on-year compared to January–September 2024. For January–September 2025, the depreciation and amortization affecting current period profits amounted to RMB 19,628,322,900, an increase of 75.94% year-on-year compared to RMB 11,156,126,600 for January–September 2024, which had a certain impact on the Company's cost of sales and period expenses. For January–September 2025, the Company's period expenses remained high due to the impact of continued significant R&D investment, additional depreciation and amortization, and increased share-based payments. For January–September 2025, as the Company's operating performance continued to improve, the loss amounts for operating profit, total profit, net profit, net profit attributable to owners of the parent company, and net profit attributable to owners of the parent company after deducting non-recurring gains and losses all narrowed further on a year-on-year basis, while EBITDA increased significantly year-on-year.

Section III. Risk Factors

When evaluating and assessing the value of the Company's shares, investors should, in addition to carefully reading the other information provided in this Prospectus, pay particular attention to the following risk factors.

I. Risks Related to the Issuer

(I) The DRAM industry is characterized by high capital intensity and high technological barriers. During the Reporting Period, the Company was in a stage of rapid capacity build-up and ramp-up, while continuously increasing R&D investment. The Company has not yet achieved profitability during the Reporting Period and has accumulated unrecovered losses.

Please refer to "Section 2: Overview," "I. Notice of Material Matters," "(I) Special Risk Warnings" of this Prospectus.

(II) Risk of Product and Technology R&D Falling Short of Expectations

Please refer to "Section 2: Overview," "I. Notice of Material Matters," "(I) Special Risk Warnings" of this Prospectus.

(III) Risk of Talent Shortage or Attrition

The DRAM industry in which the Company operates is a talent-intensive industry. From DRAM research and development to production, sales, and operational management, personnel at every stage are required to possess solid professional foundations and long-term accumulated experience. Excellent industry talent is an important foundation for the Company's survival and development, and an important guarantee for the Company to sustain its competitive advantages.

The Company has assembled a team of technical R&D and operational management professionals with extensive semiconductor industry experience, and has accumulated deep technical expertise and rich experience in DRAM product design, process development, manufacturing, quality control, and operational management. The Company places great importance on talent management, has established a comprehensive talent and compensation management system, and employs diversified methods such as equity incentives to effectively retain and attract outstanding talent. In recent years, with the support of national policies, the number of integrated circuit enterprises has grown rapidly, the demand for semiconductor industry talent has been increasing day by day, and the supply of excellent industry talent is far short of demand. In the future, if the Company is unable to continuously attract and retain outstanding talent, it may result in the loss of the Company's core talent, which could in turn affect the conduct of the Company's important R&D and innovation activities, and have an adverse impact on the Company's future business development.

(IV) Risks of Technology Leakage and Intellectual Property Protection and Disputes

Through years of continuous technological accumulation, the Company has mastered multiple core technologies related to DRAM, covering multiple stages including DRAM product design, manufacturing, packaging and testing, and module design and application, and has formed a large amount of intellectual property such as technical know-how in production processes, patents, and integrated circuit layout designs. The Company has consistently attached great importance to the protection of its core technologies, has established comprehensive information confidentiality systems, and has stipulated relevant provisions regarding ownership of inventions and works in the course of employment, confidentiality, and non-compete clauses in labor contracts and confidentiality agreements with relevant technical personnel. However, due to inherent limitations of technical confidentiality measures, the risk of key personnel turnover, and other uncontrollable external factors, in the future the Company may still face the risk of core technology leakage due to loss of core technical personnel, inadequate information management, or external theft. Furthermore, it cannot be ruled out that in the future course of business operations, disputes, controversies, or litigation related to technology and intellectual property may arise with competitors, which could have an adverse impact on the Company's market competitiveness and production and business operations.

(V) Risk of High Customer Concentration

During each period of the Reporting Period, the aggregate sales to the top five customers of the Issuer's principal business as a percentage of principal business revenue for the respective period were 69.43%, 74.12%, 67.30%, and 59.99%, respectively. Direct customers are primarily well-known distributors in the semiconductor industry, while end customers are primarily large manufacturers in downstream application fields such as servers, mobile devices, and personal computers, including Alibaba Cloud, ByteDance, Tencent, Lenovo, Xiaomi, Transsion, Honor, OPPO, vivo, and others. The Company's customer concentration is relatively high, primarily because the downstream markets for servers, mobile devices, personal computers and others that the Company serves have relatively large and concentrated demand, and the Company mainly adopts a distribution model through a small number of distributors to reach end customers. If in the future the market demand of major customers declines significantly due to adverse external factors, it may have a certain adverse impact on the Company's ability to continue as a going concern and the growth of its future performance.

(VI) Risk of Fluctuation in Gross Margin of Principal Business

During each period of the Reporting Period, the gross margin of the Company's principal business was -3.67%, -2.19%, 5.00%, and 12.72%, respectively. During the Reporting Period, on the one hand, the Company's product manufacturing costs were high, affected by factors including relatively high depreciation and amortization resulting from the Company's continuous large-scale fixed asset and R&D investment, and the economies of scale not yet having fully manifested. On the other hand, the Company's process technology level still lags behind Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix, and Micron Technology to a certain extent, and its product mix is in a process of continuous optimization; as a result, the Company's gross margin level remains lower compared to the world's top three manufacturers. In addition, compounded by product price fluctuations caused by DRAM industry cycles, the Company's gross margin experienced certain fluctuations during the Reporting Period.

If memory chip market prices decline further in the future and the Company's economies of scale fail to materialize over the long term, the Company's gross margin may face the risk of further decline, which could have an adverse impact on the Company's future ability to continue as a going concern.

(VII) Risk of Large Fixed Asset Construction Investment and Depreciation Amounts

Please refer to "Section 2: Overview," "I. Notice of Material Matters," "(I) Special Risk Warnings" of this Prospectus.

(VIII) Risk of Large R&D Investment, Large Intangible Asset Amortization Amounts, and Impairment

The Company places great importance on the independent R&D of core technologies. During the Reporting Period, the cumulative R&D investment was RMB 18,867,482,300, accounting for 33.11% of cumulative operating revenue, representing a high level of R&D investment intensity. During each period of the Reporting Period, the amortization of intangible assets provisioned was RMB 978,279,700, RMB 1,343,019,200, RMB 1,431,906,600, and RMB 902,886,500, respectively, showing an upward trend. During the Reporting Period, the capitalized amounts of R&D expenditure were RMB 1,708,604,000, RMB 150,000,000, RMB 1,734,007,800, and RMB 0, respectively, and the capitalization ratios of R&D expenditure were 40.73%, 3.21%, 27.34%, and 0, respectively. The amounts of capitalized R&D expenditure and self-developed intangible assets formed, as well as the amortization amounts, are all substantial. If the Company is adversely affected by factors such as R&D progress falling short of expectations, disruptive changes in relevant technologies, or significant changes in market demand, the relevant intangible assets may face significant impairment risk, which could in turn have an adverse impact on the Company's operating performance.

(IX) Risk of Inventory Write-Down

Please refer to "Section 2: Overview," "I. Notice of Material Matters," "(I) Special Risk Warnings" of this Prospectus.

(X) Risk of Fluctuation in Net Cash Flow from Operating Activities

During the Reporting Period, the net cash flows from operating activities were RMB -1,957,166,500, RMB -7,271,620,700, RMB 6,897,458,500, and RMB 4,250,519,000, respectively. The cash and cash equivalents balances at the end of each period were RMB 31,781,364,400, RMB 31,112,611,600, RMB 42,508,218,600, and RMB 37,270,728,800, respectively. During fiscal years 2022 and 2023, the Company's net cash flow from operating activities was negative, turning positive in 2024. In the future, if the Company's operating conditions or the external industry and market experience volatility and cannot be continuously improved, the Company's working capital turnover will face certain liquidity risks, which may constrain the Company's business development.

(XI) Risk of Having No Controlling Shareholder or Actual Controller

Please refer to "Section 2: Overview," "I. Notice of Material Matters," "(I) Special Risk Warnings" of this Prospectus.

(XII) Environmental Protection Risks

Wastewater, waste gas, and solid waste are generated in the Company's production and business operations, and the Company is required to comply with relevant laws and regulations on environmental protection. The Company actively fulfills its environmental protection responsibilities in accordance with relevant regulations, improves environmental protection measures, and has established strict environmental protection systems. However, if the Company experiences environmental pollution incidents in the future due to failures in environmental protection facilities or other reasons, it may be subject to administrative penalties from relevant authorities, which could have an adverse impact on the Company's production and business operations. At the same time, if the state or local governments introduce more stringent environmental protection requirements, the Company may be required to invest corresponding funds to upgrade and renovate existing environmental protection facilities.

(XIII) Work Safety Risks

The Company's production involves the operation of certain mechanical equipment, and some of the raw materials required carry certain hazards, placing high requirements on the technical skills and operational process knowledge of operators. The Company places great importance on work safety, has established comprehensive work safety management standards, has conducted rigorous training for operators, and has established a scientific work safety management system. However, if in the future the Company's work safety management system has loopholes, management is non-compliant, or production personnel fail to strictly follow work safety regulations during the production process, the Company faces the risk of work safety accidents, resulting in employee casualties, property losses, production line shutdowns, and administrative penalties from relevant authorities, which would have an adverse impact on the Company's production and business operations.

II. Industry-Related Risks

(I) Risk of Fluctuation in the Company's Product Sales Prices and Operating Performance Due to Macroeconomic Volatility and Industry Cyclicality

Please refer to "Section 2: Overview," "I. Notice of Material Matters," "(I) Special Risk Warnings" of this Prospectus.

(II) Risk of Supply Chain Instability Caused by International Trade Friction

Please refer to "Section 2: Overview," "I. Notice of Material Matters," "(I) Special Risk Warnings" of this Prospectus.

(III) Market Competition Risk

Looking at the history of industry development, the DRAM industry evolved from dozens of enterprises in the early stages of development in the 1980s to a situation where Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix, and Micron Technology—the three international DRAM manufacturers—have long held over 90% of the global market share, with the current market exhibiting a highly concentrated structure. Compared to the leading international manufacturers, the Company still has a certain gap in terms of overall scale, technological accumulation, and customer resources.

The DRAM market has the characteristics of a commodity with strong cyclicality. In a fiercely competitive market environment, if the Company fails to correctly grasp the trend of industrial iteration and technological development, and to promptly respond to market demand by upgrading technology, adjusting capacity, and improving product performance and service quality, it will be difficult for the Company to maintain sustainable competitiveness in the highly concentrated market, which would in turn have an adverse impact on the Company's future business prospects and market position.

III. Other Risks

(I) Risk of Proceeds Investment Projects Falling Short of Expectations in Implementation

The feasibility analysis of the investment projects for the proceeds from this offering was made based on current domestic and international market conditions, industry development trends, and technological levels. However, the aforementioned projects require a certain construction period, and once significant changes occur in the market environment, technology, management, talent, or other aspects, the projects may be adversely affected during implementation by changes in the market environment, industrial policies, and project management, as well as the sales conditions of products and services. This could affect the implementation progress of the projects, causing the actual performance of the projects to deviate from the Company's projections, and the Company faces the risk that the implementation results of the proceeds investment projects may fall short of expectations.

(II) Risk of Newly Added Expenses and Depreciation and Amortization from Proceeds Investment Projects Affecting the Company's Operating Performance

Please refer to "Section 2: Overview," "I. Notice of Material Matters," "(I) Special Risk Warnings" of this Prospectus.

(III) Risk of Issuance Failure

The Company is applying for an initial public offering of shares and listing on the STAR Market. The results of the offering will be affected by multiple internal and external factors, including the domestic and international macroeconomic environment, the overall condition of the securities market, the Company's operating performance, and investors' assessment of the industry and the Company's value. After the offering price of the Company's shares has been determined, if the number of shares subscribed by offline investors is less than the initial offline offering volume, the offering shall be suspended in accordance with the relevant provisions of the Shanghai Stock Exchange's Implementation Rules for Issuance and Underwriting of Initial Public Offerings. After the suspension, within the validity period of the registration decision granted by the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) and on the premise that post-meeting matters supervision requirements are satisfied, the Company is required to file with the Shanghai Stock Exchange before recommencing the offering. If the Company fails to complete the offering within the validity period of the registration decision granted by the CSRC, the Company will face the risk of issuance failure.

2. Establishment of the Joint Stock Company

On May 13, 2023, Deloitte issued Audit Report No. "德师报（审）字（23）第 S00406 号". As of March 31, 2023, the audited net asset value of Ruili Integrated (单体, i.e., standalone basis) was RMB 67,062,605,700 (6,706,260.57 万元).

On May 13, 2023, Zhongshui Zhiyuan Asset Appraisal Co., Ltd. (中水致远资产评估有限公司) issued Asset Appraisal Report No. "中水致远评报字[2023]第 020324 号". As of the appraisal base date of March 31, 2023, the appraised net asset value of the Company was RMB 74,991,442,500 (7,499,144.25 万元).

On June 6, 2023, Ruili Integrated convened a shareholders' meeting and deliberated and approved the "Proposal Regarding the Overall Conversion of the Company into a Joint Stock Company." The Company was converted into a joint stock company in the manner of a promoter establishment. Based on the net assets of RMB 67,062,605,743.61 as of March 31, 2023 as audited by Deloitte, the Company's share capital was converted to RMB 53,633,000,000 (5,363,300.00 万元), with a par value of RMB 1 per share. The portion of net assets exceeding the share capital was recorded in capital surplus.

On June 26, 2023, all shareholders of the Company signed the "Promoter Agreement for the Joint Establishment of CXMT Corporation (长鑫科技集团股份有限公司)," agreeing to jointly establish the joint stock company as promoters, and stipulating the share capital and shareholding ratios, the rights and obligations of each promoter, and related matters concerning the preparation and establishment of the Issuer, as well as liability for breach of contract. On the same day, the Company held its founding meeting and the First Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders for 2023, resolving to change the Company's name to "CXMT Corporation (长鑫科技集团股份有限公司)," to change the registered capital of the joint stock company to RMB 53,633,000,000 (5,363,300.00 万元), and to convert Ruili Integrated's net assets of RMB 67,062,605,743.61 as of the conversion base date of March 31, 2023, as confirmed by Deloitte's audit, into the joint stock company's share capital of RMB 53,633,000,000 (5,363,300.00 万元) at a ratio of 1:0.7997 (rounded to four decimal places). The portion of net assets exceeding the share capital was recorded in the joint stock company's capital surplus. The joint stock company's share capital is divided into shares of equal value, with a total number of 53,633,000,000 shares (5,363,300 万股), a par value of RMB 1 per share, all being RMB ordinary shares.

On June 27, 2023, the Company received a business license for a joint stock company with the unified social credit code "91340100MA2MWUT60Q," issued by the Hefei Market Supervision Administration.

On June 28, 2023, Deloitte issued Capital Verification Report No. "德师报（验）字（23）第 00178 号" for "CXMT Corporation (Preparatory) (长鑫科技集团股份有限公司（筹）)," confirming that as of June 26, 2023, the Company had received the full share capital of RMB 53,633,000,000 (5,363,300.00 万元) contributed by all promoters in the form of the net assets of Ruili Integrated held by them.

3. Unrecovered Losses Existing at the Time of Overall Conversion

(1) Reasons for Negative Undistributed Profits as of the Share Reform Base Date

On May 13, 2023, Deloitte audited the financial statements of Ruili Integrated as of March 31, 2023 and issued Audit Report No. "德师报（审）字（23）第 S00406 号." As audited, the net asset value of the parent company of Ruili Integrated as of March 31, 2023 was RMB 67,062,605,700 (6,706,260.57 万元), and the undistributed profits were RMB -2,591,813,300 (-259,181.33 万元).

The Company had negative undistributed profits primarily because, during the Reporting Period, the Company was in the production capacity ramp-up stage, with large and continuously increasing depreciation and amortization amounts in the early stage of production lines, while at the same time the Company's product prices declined due to the impact of the memory chip market environment and industry cyclicality, and asset impairment losses provisioned increased. The combined impact of internal and external factors resulted in negative undistributed profits at the time of the overall conversion.

(2) Status of Elimination of Negative Undistributed Profits, Changes and Development Trends After the Overall Conversion, Consistency with Changes in Profitability During the Reporting Period, and Impact on Future Profitability

Through the overall conversion, the Company eliminated the accumulated unrecovered losses of RMB -2,591,813,300 (-259,181.33 万元) recorded on the parent company's books as of the share reform base date. As of June 30, 2025, the undistributed profits at the consolidated financial statement level were RMB -40,857,338,700 (-4,085,733.87 万元), and the situation of negative undistributed profits has not yet been eliminated.

After the completion of the overall conversion, the Company continued to expand its business, with existing production capacity gradually being released and newly constructed production capacity gradually being completed, and the Company's product competitiveness and industry influence continuously strengthened. At the same time, the Company has intensified its efforts to attract talent, strengthened team stability, continuously increased R&D investment, and actively planned and launched more advanced generation products such as DDR5. The situation of negative undistributed profits has no adverse impact on the Company's cash flow, business expansion, talent attraction, team stability, R&D investment, strategic investment, and sustainability of production and business operations, and does not constitute a material adverse impact on the Company's future ability to continue as a going concern and profitability.

(3) Specific Plan for the Overall Conversion and Corresponding Accounting Treatment

Based on the net assets of RMB 67,062,605,743.61 (6,706,260.57 万元) as of the conversion base date of March 31, 2023, as confirmed by Deloitte's Audit Report No. "德师报（审）字（23）第 S00406 号," the Company converted its net assets into the joint stock company's share capital of RMB 53,633,000,000 (5,363,300 万元) at a ratio of 1:0.7997 (rounded to four decimal places). The portion of net assets exceeding the share capital was recorded in the joint stock company's capital surplus. Through the overall conversion, the Company eliminated the accumulated unrecovered losses of RMB -2,591,813,300 (-259,181.33 万元) recorded on the parent company's books as of the share reform base date.

(5) June 2024 — Eighth Capital Increase

On February 22, 2024, Changxin Technology (长鑫科技) convened the First Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders of 2024 and passed a resolution approving the Company to proceed with a financing round to be completed in stages. On March 31, 2024, the Company convened the Second Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders of 2024 and passed a resolution confirming that the first stage financing amount would be RMB 5,550,000,000 (RMB 555,000.00 ten-thousand yuan) and the second stage financing amount would be RMB 5,250,000,000 (RMB 525,000.00 ten-thousand yuan). Upon completion of both stages of this financing, the Company's registered capital was increased from RMB 53,633,000,000 (RMB 5,363,300.00 ten-thousand yuan) to RMB 57,770,942,200 (RMB 5,777,094.22 ten-thousand yuan), an increase of RMB 4,137,942,200 (RMB 413,794.22 ten-thousand yuan) in registered capital, all of which was subscribed in cash by investors; the portion of the capital contribution in excess of the subscribed registered capital was recorded as the Company's capital reserve.

(7) June 2025 — Eighth Share Transfer of Changxin Technology

On May 8, 2025, Glades View and Guangzhou Xinde (广州信德) entered into the Share Transfer Agreement in respect of Changxin Technology Group Co., Ltd. (长鑫科技集团股份有限公司), pursuant to which Glades View agreed to transfer to Guangzhou Xinde (广州信德) the registered capital of RMB 103,016,700 (RMB 10,301.67 ten-thousand yuan) held in Changxin Technology (corresponding to 0.1783% of the shares) at a price of RMB 267,500,000 (RMB

All property interests in Hefei Shuimu (representing a property interest ratio of 3.322%, corresponding to a capital contribution of RMB 25,000,000) were transferred to Lü Dalong. Pursuant to the provisions of the Transfer Agreement, from the date on which the industrial and commercial change registration for the completion of this interest transfer is processed, Lü Dalong shall obtain ownership of all property interests involved in this transfer and become a partner of Hefei Shuimu. On December 17, 2025, the competent registration authority completed the industrial and commercial registration procedures for this change of partner.

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, all historical nominee shareholding arrangements that existed in the history of the Issuer have been lawfully dissolved, and there are no disputes or potential disputes regarding equity ownership among the parties to the relevant equity transfers. The Issuer's share ownership is clear and there are no nominee shareholding arrangements.

(II) Controlling Shareholders and Actual Controllers

The Company has no controlling shareholder and no actual controller. There has been no change in the actual controller in the past two years.

According to the Company's shareholding structure, the shareholders directly holding more than 5% of the Company's shares are Qinghui Jidian, Changxin Integration, Big Fund Phase II, Hefei Jixin, and Anhui Provincial Investment, holding 21.67%, 11.71%, 8.73%, 8.37%, and 7.91% of Changxin Technology's equity respectively as of the date of signing of this Prospectus. No single shareholder holds more than 50% of the shares, and the Company's shareholding structure is relatively dispersed.

In terms of board composition, the Company's Board of Directors consists of 11 members, including 4 independent directors. Among the 7 non-independent directors, Qinghui Jidian nominated 2, Big Fund Phase II nominated 2, Hefei Jixin nominated 1, Anhui Provincial Investment nominated 1, and 1 is an employee director. Pursuant to Changxin Technology's Articles of Association and the partnership agreement of Qinghui Jidian, in the past two years, Qinghui Jidian nominated 2 directors to Changxin Technology, who were respectively nominated and recommended by the executive partner Qinghui Changxin and the limited partner Changxin Integration. The actual nominators of the 7 non-independent directors of Changxin Technology's Board of Directors are: Qinghui Changxin 1 seat, Changxin Integration 1 seat, Big Fund Phase II 2 seats, Hefei Jixin 1 seat, Anhui Provincial Investment 1 seat, and employee director 1 seat. Therefore, no single shareholder is able to determine the appointment of more than half of the members of Changxin Technology's Board of Directors through actual control of voting rights.

In summary, based on the Company's shareholding structure and board composition, throughout the reporting period, the Company has had no controlling shareholder and no actual controller.

(III) Other Enterprises Controlled by Controlling Shareholders and Actual Controllers

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Company has no controlling shareholder and no actual controller; therefore, there are no other enterprises controlled by any controlling shareholder or actual controller.

Chang Xin Technology Group Co., Ltd. (长鑫科技集团股份有限公司) — Prospectus

(IX) Special Rights Arrangements Between the Company and Its Shareholders

On June 24, 2025, Chang Xin Technology entered into the Shareholders' Agreement in Respect of Chang Xin Technology Group Co., Ltd. (superseding the previously executed shareholders' agreement, hereinafter referred to as the "Shareholders' Agreement") with the Company's shareholders, which sets out the special rights of shareholders. These include provisions on "restrictions on share transfers," "right of first refusal," "co-sale rights," "preemptive rights," "anti-dilution," "most favored nation treatment," "information and inspection rights," and "special rights of the Phase II Fund"1 (hereinafter collectively referred to as the "Special Rights Provisions").

The Company and all shareholders have expressly agreed in the Shareholders' Agreement on the termination of the Special Rights Provisions, the principal terms of which are as follows:

  1. The "Special Rights of the Phase II Fund" shall automatically terminate on the date on which the Company submits its application for an initial public offering to the relevant securities regulatory authority or stock exchange.

  2. The other Special Rights Provisions, including "restrictions on share transfers," "right of first refusal," "co-sale rights," "preemptive rights," "anti-dilution," "most favored nation treatment," and "information and inspection rights," shall automatically terminate on the date on which the Company submits its application for an initial public offering to the relevant securities regulatory authority or stock exchange. At the same time, all parties agree and confirm that if such provisions automatically terminate as agreed and any of the following circumstances occurs, the relevant provisions shall immediately and automatically be reinstated and treated as if they had never been terminated, provided that the implementation of the relevant arrangements complies with the applicable laws and regulations in force at that time: (i) the Company voluntarily withdraws its listing application; or (ii) the Company's listing application is not accepted, fails to pass review, or is approved or ratified upon review but ultimately fails to successfully list and trade on the relevant stock exchange. After the automatic termination of the Special Rights Provisions and prior to their reinstatement, the rights and obligations among the parties shall be determined by the Company's articles of association; prior to the automatic termination of the Special Rights Provisions or after their reinstatement, in the event of any inconsistency between the articles of association and the key provisions, the Special Rights Provisions shall prevail.

The Special Rights Provisions agreed upon in the Shareholders' Agreement do not involve any repurchase obligation to be borne by the issuer, do not involve any change of control of the Company, are not linked to market capitalization, do not materially and adversely affect the Company's ability to continue as a going concern or otherwise materially and adversely affect investors' interests, and do not violate the provisions of the Regulatory Rules Application Guidelines — Issuance Category No. 4.

On December 5, 2025, all shareholders of the Company executed the Agreement on Termination of Special Rights in Respect of Chang Xin Technology Group Co., Ltd. Pursuant to the termination agreement signed by the parties, the Special Rights Provisions stipulated in the aforementioned Shareholders' Agreement shall, as of the date on which the Company formally submits its application for an initial public offering of A-shares and listing to the stock exchange and such application is accepted, be void ab initio and irrevocably terminated. There are no circumstances that would constitute a material adverse effect on the issuer or seriously impair investors' interests. This is in compliance with the relevant requirements of the Regulatory Rules Application Guidelines — Issuance Category No. 4.

1 Pursuant to Article 18.14 of the Shareholders' Agreement: All parties unanimously agree that, prior to the Company achieving a listing, the following matters shall be deliberated by the shareholders' meeting and must be approved by National Integrated Circuit Industry Investment Fund Phase II Co., Ltd. ("Phase II Fund") before being adopted: (a) any amendment, modification, termination, deletion or re-execution in any form of any provision in the transaction documents signed by the Phase II Fund (referring to this agreement and the articles of association) relating to the rights, priorities, privileges, powers or provisions in favor of the Phase II Fund; (b) the election and replacement of directors nominated by the Phase II Fund, and any change in the number of board members prior to the Company's next round of financing; (c) the merger, division, liquidation, dissolution, termination, suspension of business, or change of form or nature of the Company, or any resolution with respect to events that may lead to the dissolution, cessation of business, bankruptcy or liquidation of the Company.

(X) Circumstances of Public Sale of Shares by the Issuer's Shareholders

This public offering does not involve any circumstances in which existing shareholders who have held their shares for at least 36 months publicly sell shares to investors.

XI. Information on Directors, Supervisors, Senior Management, and Core Technical Personnel

(I) Basic Information on Directors, Audit Committee, Senior Management, and Core Technical Personnel

The Company's Board of Directors is composed of 11 members, including 4 independent directors. Directors are elected by the shareholders' meeting for a term of three years per term and may be re-elected upon the expiration of each term. Independent directors serve a term of three years and may be re-elected upon the expiration of each term, but the total consecutive term shall not exceed six years.

5. Concluding Opinion on Personnel Changes Over the Past Two Years

There have been no material adverse changes in the Company's directors, supervisors, senior management, or core technical personnel over the past two years. The changes were primarily attributable to adjustments in nominations and appointments by shareholder entities, the Company's need to further improve its governance structure, and the abolition of the Supervisory Board in accordance with applicable regulations. The Issuer's core management team and core technical personnel are stable and are responsible for strategic planning, research and development, production, sales, financial, and other operational management activities, and participate in the Company's business decisions. Directors and senior management who departed in the past two years will not have a material adverse impact on the Company's production and operations.

Changes in the Company's directors, supervisors, and senior management have all been conducted in accordance with the procedures stipulated in the Company Law and the Articles of Association, and such changes have not had a material adverse impact on the Company's corporate governance structure or the continuity of its business policies.
