(2) Comparison of Technical Parameters

In terms of specific technical parameters, since all manufacturers design and manufacture standard DRAM products in accordance with JEDEC standards, products of the same generation from different manufacturers strictly adhere to industry specifications in key indicators such as speed, bandwidth, and voltage, and exhibit a high degree of consistency in core performance parameters, interface protocols, and basic functions.

(IV) Competitive Strengths and Weaknesses

1. Competitive Strengths of the Company

(1) Continuous R&D Innovation and Rapid Technology Iteration Capability

As computing and processing chip performance continues to improve, increasingly higher demands are placed on DRAM, requiring DRAM products to continuously iterate and upgrade toward greater storage density, faster transmission speeds, and lower power consumption. Therefore, continuously iterating products and process technologies while maintaining industry-leading technology levels is the key to surviving in the DRAM industry and maintaining product competitiveness. The Company has always adhered to the path of independent R&D and has accelerated technological innovation through generation-skipping R&D, rapidly completing mass production from the first-generation process technology platform to the fourth-generation process technology platform, as well as product coverage and iteration from DDR4 and LPDDR4X to DDR5 and LPDDR5/5X. Currently, the core products and process technologies have reached internationally advanced levels. In addition, companies operating under the IDM model need to invest large amounts of R&D resources simultaneously in product design, process technology, packaging and testing, and module design and manufacturing. Only when the technology and innovation capabilities of all business segments "advance in tandem" can comprehensive competitiveness be effectively maintained. After years of development, the Company has built a complete core technology system across all business segments including DRAM product design, manufacturing processes, packaging and testing, and module design and application. At the same time, the Company has formed comprehensive independent intellectual property rights and accumulated extensive experience in rapid technology iteration and industrialization.

(2) Continuously Increasing Production Capacity with Increasingly Enhanced Economies of Scale

DRAM is a highly standardized product in the semiconductor industry, and its capacity construction requires enormous capital investment. The cost advantages brought by economies of scale have become one of the Company's core competencies. The Company's production capacity ranks first in China and fourth globally. As the Company's currently planned capacity construction investment is gradually completed and capacity rapidly ramps up, the business scale effect gradually becomes apparent. Combined with the effective collaboration of all business segments under the IDM model and efficient cost management, the Company's product cost advantages and price competitiveness will continue to strengthen, facilitating the Company's further acceleration of market penetration in the future.

(3) A Rich Product Portfolio That Continuously Iterates and Upgrades, with Comprehensive DRAM Product Solution Capabilities

Different application scenarios have significantly different requirements for DRAM interfaces, capacities, speeds, bandwidths, and product packaging forms. Only by establishing a sufficiently rich product matrix can the Company better compete with internationally leading DRAM manufacturers. The Company closely follows industry-leading technologies and downstream application market development trends, and through continuously strengthening R&D investment and relentless technological breakthroughs, has completed the product layout for the DDR series and LPDDR series, with product speed, power consumption, and other performance parameters reaching internationally advanced levels. Among them, the Company's DDR series products cover the two mainstream product generations of DDR4 and DDR5, and can provide DRAM module products in multiple capacity specifications including 8GB, 16GB, 32GB, 64GB, and 96GB, effectively meeting the demand in application markets such as servers and personal computers. In terms of LPDDR series products, the Company has currently launched LPDDR4X and LPDDR5/5X products, and offers multiple capacity options including below 8GB, 8GB, 12GB, and 16GB, which are widely used in mobile devices such as mobile phones and tablets, other small electronic devices with relatively strict power consumption requirements, and the smart vehicle sector. In addition, the Company is also actively enriching and upgrading existing products by launching upgraded products with higher speeds and larger capacities, while also laying out next-generation products to better meet market demands. With its continuously expanding product portfolio, the Company is able to provide customers with diversified product solutions and has become a major competitor in the global DRAM market.

(4) Excellent Coordination and Large-Scale Production Operations Management Capabilities

The DRAM industry is characterized by high product complexity and fast iteration speed, a large variety of raw materials, complex production processes, and high manufacturing difficulty. Downstream customers also have high demands for DRAM product quality, stability, on-time delivery, and stable supply. Excellent coordination and large-scale production operations management capabilities are an important manifestation of a DRAM company's core competitiveness. After years of development, the Company has built an efficient R&D and innovation mechanism to ensure efficient integration between technology R&D and product mass production, effectively improving the efficiency of technology industrialization. On the other hand, the Company possesses strong supply chain integration and management capabilities, able to effectively ensure that the supply of various equipment and raw materials dynamically matches the Company's production needs. In addition, through the introduction of automated, informatized, and intelligent production systems and process optimization, the Company has effectively improved production efficiency and reduced unit costs, while also ensuring the precision and consistency of process technologies across the Company's factories in various locations. Excellent operations management capabilities are an important foundation for the Company's rapid development and maintenance of long-term stable supply.

(5) Close Collaboration with Upstream and Downstream Industries, Building a Powerful Industrial Ecosystem

As the leading enterprise (链主企业) in China's DRAM supply chain, based on its own powerful industrial influence, the Company has established close strategic cooperative relationships with upstream suppliers and downstream customers, actively integrating industrial ecosystem resources and jointly building a mutually interdependent industrial ecosystem, laying a solid foundation for the Company's continued business development and active participation in global competition. On one hand, through long-term development, the Company has established stable cooperative relationships with upstream suppliers such as EDA vendors, material manufacturers, and equipment and component manufacturers. By deepening supply chain cooperation, the Company actively promotes the diversification and localization of the supply chain, enhancing supply chain security capabilities, providing strong guarantees for the Company's continued capacity expansion and process technology upgrades. On the other hand, the Company has established close cooperative relationships with downstream customers such as industry-leading mobile device manufacturers, server manufacturers, and memory module manufacturers, has earned high recognition from many customers, and has established a good market brand, laying a solid foundation for the Company to strengthen market penetration and increase its global market share.

(6) A Professional, Pragmatic, and Experienced Technology and Management Team

The Company has assembled a professional, pragmatic, and enterprising core R&D and operations management team with extensive industry experience. The core leaders of the Company's management team have more than 25 years of experience in the semiconductor industry, with profound industry insights and outstanding strategic resolve, jointly guiding the Company's growth and strategy execution. The Company actively cultivates an entrepreneurial and innovation culture, encourages technological innovation, and has attracted talent from globally renowned semiconductor companies. The Company's technology and R&D team is composed of expert teams with extensive semiconductor industry experience from both China and abroad, with deep technical backgrounds and extensive management experience in DRAM product design, process development, production manufacturing, and quality control. The Company also attaches great importance to talent cultivation and the tiered deployment and continuous development of the team. As of the end of June 2025, R&D personnel account for more than 30% of the Company's total workforce. The strong talent and team reserves are an important foundation for the Company to maintain long-term competitiveness.

2. Competitive Weaknesses of the Company

(1) Relatively Late Start in Development, with a Certain Gap Remaining Compared to International Leading Companies

The DRAM industry has long been dominated by leading manufacturers such as Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix, and Micron Technology. The Company started its development relatively late, and compared to the aforementioned peers in the same industry, there is still a certain gap in terms of revenue scale, R&D and capital investment scale, production capacity scale, and product portfolio. The aforementioned companies have undergone decades of development and have formed deep technological accumulations in areas such as DRAM product design and manufacturing, possessing first-mover technological advantages and certain cost advantages. Compared with the aforementioned manufacturers, the Company is currently still in an early stage of development and faces relatively greater competitive pressure.

(2) Certain International Supply Chain Resources Are Restricted

In recent years, due to international geopolitical factors, global semiconductor industry division of labor and cooperation has become increasingly restricted. In the current challenging industrial development environment, the Company faces certain limitations in accessing certain international supply chain resources and conducting international industrial cooperation.

(3) Restricted Financing Channels

The DRAM industry is a typical heavy capital investment industry. The construction and operation of production capacity requires substantial capital investment, and continuous product, technology, and process iteration also requires sustained high R&D investment. International DRAM manufacturers such as Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix, and Micron Technology are all publicly listed companies, able to conveniently conduct capital operations to support their business development. Compared with the aforementioned companies, the Company's financing channels remain relatively restricted. In recent years, the Company has continuously strengthened capacity construction and expansion, product and technology upgrades, and market penetration. As the Company's business has developed rapidly, the problem of insufficient capital arising from restricted financing channels has become one of the main bottlenecks constraining the Company's development. The Company's business growth urgently requires capital support.

VIII. Core Technologies and R&D of the Issuer

(I) Core Technology Status of the Company

1. Core Technology Overview

The Company operates under the IDM (Integrated Device Manufacturer) model. Its core technology capabilities are primarily reflected in two areas: process technology and product technology. Leading product technology is an important foundation for the Company to respond rapidly to the market, continuously optimize and upgrade products, and bring forth new offerings. Process technology is an important support for the effective commercialization of the Company's product technology, and also an important guarantee for the continuous improvement of product quality, performance, and cost. Process technology and product technology complement each other and together constitute the Company's core technological competitiveness.

The Company has deeply focused on the design and manufacturing of DRAM memory chips. Through continuous R&D investment and technological exploration, the Company has mastered numerous core process and product technologies that have reached internationally advanced levels, covering all business segments including DRAM product design, manufacturing processes, packaging and testing, module design, and applications.

As of June 30, 2025, the Company holds a total of 3,116 domestic patents (including 2,348 invention patents) and 2,473 overseas patents. The Company's core technologies are primarily formed through independent research and development. The Company's R&D team has developed the existing core technology platform through research and innovation, which has been extensively applied in the Company's mass production of products. The details of the Company's core technologies are as follows:

(1) DRAM Process Technology

No. Core Technology Name Process Technology Features and Indicators of Advancement Technology Source Level of Technology Advancement Current Stage
1 1st Generation Process Technology Platform Applies low-leakage, high-performance, high-density memory array structure design and manufacturing technology; introduces buried wordline, saddle-fin active area, bitline, contact plug, landing pad, and honeycomb-arrangement capacitor structures to enhance memory array performance. Independent R&D Domestically Leading Iteration completed; tape-out discontinued
2 3rd Generation Process Technology Platform 1. Applies multiple self-aligned exposure and other lithography process technologies to achieve finer pattern transfer and reduce product size;
2. Applies refined chemical mechanical polishing (CMP) processes to continuously improve the interface uniformity of each step in DRAM production, enhance polishing precision, reduce scratches, particles, and other defects, thereby improving product yield.		 Independent R&D Domestically Leading Mass Production
3 4th Generation Process Technology Platform 1. Adopts low-leakage, high-performance control transistor device design and manufacturing technology to achieve high-performance, high-reliability peripheral control capability;
2. Applies various refined thin-film deposition processes to meet the requirements for high planarity and high uniformity of thin films with different properties in the DRAM manufacturing process, improving overall chip performance;
3. Improves cleaning process procedures to effectively reduce particle residue and prevent adhesion of small-scale structures caused by surface tension, meeting the sharply increased demand for cleaning steps and cleaning capability due to process technology advancement, thereby improving product yield;
4. Adopts process control and advanced metrology technology to meet the stringent requirements of advanced DRAM manufacturing for controlling critical dimensions, material properties, product defects, and manufacturing efficiency.		 Independent R&D Internationally Advanced Mass Production

3. Protection of Core Technologies

(1) Intellectual Property Protection

The Company places great importance on the protection of its core technologies and has established a relatively complete patent portfolio for the aforementioned core technologies, building a scientifically sound and comprehensive intellectual property management system and formulating a full lifecycle management system for intellectual property. In accordance with the relevant systems and procedures, the Company's Intellectual Property Department centrally manages patent applications and other intellectual property rights (such as integrated circuit layout designs) filed with the National Intellectual Property Administration, and distributes bonuses to patent inventors in accordance with the provisions of the "Intellectual Property Award Management Measures," ensuring that R&D results receive timely and effective legal protection. If R&D results are not suitable for public disclosure, they may, upon approval through deliberation, be classified as trade secrets for protection. Through the effective operation of the Company's intellectual property management system and the implementation of intellectual property-related rules and regulations, the Company has clarified the ownership of its intellectual property rights and effectively protected its core technologies.

(2) Confidentiality and Non-Compete System

In order to ensure that the ownership of work-for-hire products is clear and unambiguous, prevent the leakage of technical secrets due to employee turnover, and guard against intellectual property disputes with employees, the Company stipulates corresponding ownership of work-for-hire inventions and works, confidentiality, and non-compete clauses in labor contracts, confidentiality agreements, and other relevant agreements with its technical personnel, requiring them to bear confidentiality obligations with respect to technical secrets and business information arising in the course of their work. With respect to the resignation of core technical personnel, corresponding advance notice periods and non-compete periods shall be stipulated. Personnel subject to non-compete restrictions shall not, during the non-compete period, establish, operate, or participate in any entity competing with the Issuer, nor shall they work for, provide services to, or render consulting work to such entities.

(II) Scientific Research Capabilities and Achievements

1. Major Scientific Research Projects Undertaken

As the largest, most technologically advanced, and most comprehensively deployed integrated DRAM R&D, design, and manufacturing enterprise in China, the Company possesses strong scientific research capabilities and technological innovation capacity in the relevant business fields, and has long undertaken and participated in various major scientific research projects. During the Reporting Period, the Company participated in multiple major scientific research projects.

Pollutant Category Main Pollutants Treatment Methods
Wastewater General acid-alkaline wastewater, fluorine-containing wastewater, ammonia-containing wastewater, and other wastewater Production wastewater is collected separately and conveyed to the Company's wastewater treatment station for centralized treatment. After meeting the standards for connection to municipal sewage treatment plants, it is discharged together with domestic sewage into the municipal sewage network and ultimately enters the sewage treatment plant.
Waste Gas Acid waste gas, alkaline waste gas, organic waste gas, and other waste gases
  1. The acid waste gas treatment system uses alkaline liquid spray to treat acid waste gas, or collects it through fume hood ducts and processes it through a two-stage activated carbon system;
  2. The alkaline waste gas treatment system uses acid liquid spray to treat alkaline waste gas;
  3. The organic waste gas treatment system uses a zeolite concentrating rotor incineration system to treat organic waste gas, or collects it through fume hood ducts and processes it through a two-stage activated carbon system;
  4. The process tail gas treatment system uses "dry adsorption" source treatment devices and "combustion + water scrubbing" source treatment devices to treat process tail gas;
  5. Boiler flue gas is treated using low-nitrogen burners.
Solid Waste Hazardous waste (such as sludge, waste acid, waste alkali, etc.) and general solid waste (such as general waste packaging materials, metal scraps, etc.)
  1. For hazardous waste, the Company has established dedicated hazardous waste storage facilities for collection and storage, and entrusts qualified third parties to carry out transfer and disposal in accordance with standardized declaration and approval procedures;
  2. For general solid waste, the Company sorts and categorizes it before selling it for comprehensive utilization, or entrusts qualified entities to sell it for comprehensive utilization or transfer for disposal.
Noise Factory boundary noise Measures such as vibration-damping platforms and workshop sound insulation are employed.

The Issuer's main wastewater treatment facilities include ammonia-containing wastewater treatment systems, fluorine-containing wastewater treatment systems, and organic wastewater treatment systems; the main waste gas treatment facilities include acid waste gas treatment systems, alkaline waste gas treatment systems, and organic waste gas treatment systems. During the Reporting Period, the Issuer's environmental protection facilities operated properly, and there were no circumstances in which the relevant competent authorities imposed significant administrative penalties for violations of environmental protection laws and regulations. The Company's production and business operations comply with national and local environmental protection requirements.

II. Audit Opinion and Key Audit Matters of the Accounting Firm

(I) Audit Opinion

Deloitte audited the Issuer's financial statements, including the consolidated and parent company balance sheets as of 30 June 2025, 31 December 2024, 31 December 2023, and 31 December 2022; the consolidated and parent company income statements, consolidated and parent company cash flow statements, consolidated and parent company statements of changes in owners' equity, and related notes to the financial statements for the period from 1 January 2025 to 30 June 2025, the year 2024, the year 2023, and the year 2022.

Deloitte is of the opinion that the Issuer's financial statements are prepared in accordance with the Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises in all material respects and fairly present, in all material respects, the consolidated and parent company financial position of the Issuer as of 30 June 2025, 31 December 2024, 31 December 2023, and 31 December 2022, as well as the consolidated and parent company results of operations and consolidated and parent company cash flows for the period from 1 January 2025 to 30 June 2025, the year 2024, the year 2023, and the year 2022.

(II) Key Audit Matters

1. Revenue Recognition

(1) Description of the Matter

The Issuer's operating revenue is primarily derived from the sale of DRAM products, principally including the DDR series and LPDDR series. For the period from 1 January 2025 to 30 June 2025, the year 2024, the year 2023, and the year 2022, the Issuer's revenue from sales of principal DRAM products amounted to RMB 1,485,230.23 ten thousand yuan, RMB 2,297,223.19 ten thousand yuan, RMB 858,323.94 ten thousand yuan, and RMB 789,143.90 ten thousand yuan, respectively. As the Issuer's sales revenue grew rapidly during the Reporting Period and operating revenue is one of its key performance indicators, Deloitte identified DRAM product sales revenue as a key audit matter.

(2) Audit Response

With respect to the above DRAM product sales revenue, the principal audit procedures performed by Deloitte include:

  1. Understanding the key internal controls related to revenue recognition of the Issuer, and evaluating the design and implementation of such key internal controls;
  2. Reviewing the terms of significant sales contracts and assessing whether the Issuer's revenue recognition policies comply with the Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises;
  3. Performing analytical procedures on product sales revenue and gross margin during the Reporting Period, and inquiring with management about the reasons for fluctuations in sales revenue and gross margin during the Reporting Period and assessing their reasonableness;
  4. Selecting samples by customer to perform confirmation procedures on sales amounts, corresponding contract liability balances, and accounts receivable balances;
  5. Selecting samples from sales revenue transactions recorded during the Reporting Period and examining supporting documents such as sales orders, invoices, outbound delivery notes, signed receipt confirmations, and customs declarations (where applicable) to test the authenticity of sales revenue;
  6. Conducting background checks, on-site visits, or video/telephone interviews with the Issuer's key customers.

2. Capitalization of Research and Development Expenditure

(1) Description of the Matter

For the period from 1 January 2025 to 30 June 2025, the year 2024, the year 2023, and the year 2022, the research and development expenditure in the Issuer's consolidated financial statements that met the criteria for capitalization amounted to RMB 0.00 ten thousand yuan, RMB 173,400.78 ten thousand yuan, RMB 15,000.00 ten thousand yuan, and RMB 170,860.40 ten thousand yuan, respectively. As the amounts of research and development expenditure were significant during the Reporting Period, and determining whether research and development expenditure meets the criteria for capitalization requires significant accounting judgments and estimates by management, Deloitte identified the capitalization of research and development expenditure as a key audit matter.

(2) Audit Response

With respect to the capitalization of research and development expenditure, the principal audit procedures performed by Deloitte include:

  1. Understanding the key internal controls related to research and development expenditure of the Issuer, and evaluating the design and implementation of such key internal controls;
  2. Examining management-approved project development budgets, and evaluating the reasonableness of management's analysis of the expected economic benefits of the development projects by collecting relevant market information;
  3. Taking into account the Issuer's capital and technology reserves, understanding management's plans to provide funding and technical resources to support development projects and subsequent production;
  4. Examining project initiation documents, technical feasibility demonstration documents, preliminary test reports on the completion of relevant products, and management review records, and checking whether the capitalized projects have entered the development phase;
  5. Sampling research and development expenditure supporting documents to verify whether the scope of cost aggregation for research and development expenditure is appropriate, whether the measurement of research and development expenditure is true and accurate, and whether it is genuinely related to the relevant research and development activities.

3. Provision for Inventory Write-down

(1) Description of the Matter

When there is objective evidence of inventory impairment, the Issuer's management records provision for inventory write-down based on the excess of cost over the net realizable value of inventories. As of 30 June 2025, 31 December 2024, 31 December 2023, and 31 December 2022, the gross book value of inventories (excluding real estate development products/costs) reported in the Issuer's consolidated financial statements amounted to RMB 2,765,918.31 ten thousand yuan, RMB 2,062,355.75 ten thousand yuan, RMB 1,361,385.74 ten thousand yuan, and RMB 893,374.76 ten thousand yuan, respectively, and the provision for inventory write-down amounted to RMB 189,796.09 ten thousand yuan, RMB 257,386.40 ten thousand yuan, RMB 344,429.77 ten thousand yuan, and RMB 251,703.31 ten thousand yuan, respectively. As inventory amounts were significant and determining the net realizable value of inventories involves significant accounting estimates by management, Deloitte identified the provision for inventory write-down (excluding real estate development products/costs) as a key audit matter.

(2) Audit Response

The principal audit procedures performed by Deloitte with respect to the provision for inventory write-down include:

  1. Understanding the key internal controls related to the provision for inventory write-down (excluding real estate development products/costs) of the Issuer, and evaluating the design and implementation of such key internal controls;
  2. Obtaining the computation schedules for the provision for inventory write-down (excluding real estate development products/costs) and examining the calculation process; reviewing management's method of recording the provision for inventory write-down and evaluating the reasonableness of the relevant estimates used in the testing, including estimated selling price, estimated costs to be incurred to bring the inventories to completion, estimated selling expenses, and related taxes; comparing management's estimated selling price with recent actual selling prices and market information, and comparing management's estimated costs, selling expenses, and related taxes with post-period or historical actual data;
  3. In conjunction with the results of the inventory count, examining whether there are inventories at period-end with extended aging, obsolescence, or damage, and analyzing whether the provision for inventory write-down is adequate and reasonable;
  4. Examining whether information related to the provision for inventory write-down has been properly presented and disclosed in the financial statements.
