During each period of the reporting period, the Company's principal business cost comprised direct materials, direct labor, manufacturing overhead, and other costs. Other costs entirely represent the reversal of inventory write-down provisions. During the reporting period, due to the rapid capacity ramp-up and continuous technology iteration, depreciation and amortization within manufacturing overhead was at a relatively high level, resulting in relatively high unit production costs. The Company recognized inventory write-down provisions to the extent that the production cost of inventories exceeded their selling prices. Upon sale of inventories for which write-down provisions had been recognized, such provisions were reversed and offset against principal business costs.

In 2023, the amount of inventory write-down provision reversals increased substantially, primarily because the Company's sales volumes rose rapidly alongside capacity expansion, but product prices fell sharply due to industry cycle impacts during that year, leading to a significant increase in both the recognition and reversal of inventory write-down provisions. In 2024 and the first half of 2025, with the recovery of the DRAM industry and the optimization of the Company's product mix, product selling prices continued to recover; at the same time, as the effects of scale economies and lean production management became increasingly evident, unit production costs continued to decline, and the scale of inventory write-down provision reversals decreased significantly.

(VIII) Circumstances of Not Yet Achieving Profitability and the Existence of Accumulated Uncovered Losses

During the reporting period, the net profit attributable to shareholders of the parent company was RMB -8,328,003,900, RMB -16,339,777,200, RMB -7,144,887,200, and RMB -2,332,058,200, respectively. As of June 30, 2025, the Company has not yet achieved profitability and has accumulated uncovered losses.

1. Specific Reasons for Losses

The specific reasons why the Company has remained in a loss-making position throughout the reporting period are as follows:

(1) IDM enterprises require continuous and intensive capital investment; during the reporting period, the Company was in a phase of rapid capacity ramp-up, and economies of scale had not yet been fully realized.

The design and manufacturing of DRAM chips are characterized by strong coupling and technical complexity. In order to improve production efficiency, achieve mass production so as to reduce per-unit costs, and at the same time strengthen autonomy over the supply chain and enhance industry influence, the capital-intensive IDM model has become the inevitable choice for DRAM manufacturers competing internationally. In order to optimize costs and expand market share as quickly as possible, the Company rapidly built out production capacity during the reporting period. However, during the reporting period, the Company was still in a phase of rapid capacity ramp-up, and economies of scale had not yet been fully realized; the continuous expansion of fixed assets brought about a continuous increase in depreciation amounts. During the reporting period, the Company's depreciation charges on fixed assets were RMB 5,242,713,300, RMB 10,555,252,600, RMB 14,875,435,800, and RMB 11,349,488,600, respectively, which has had a significant impact on the Company's profitability.

(2) The DRAM industry is characterized by high R&D expenditure; the Company's technology process iterations and product mix optimization have led to continuously increasing R&D expenses, while the overall effect of gross margin improvement has yet to become further apparent.

In order to continuously respond to and satisfy downstream market demand and ensure product competitiveness, DRAM enterprises are required to make continuous and substantial R&D investments to iterate on product technologies and manufacturing processes and to continuously optimize their product mix. During the reporting period, the Company completed the R&D and mass production of its third-generation and fourth-generation technology platforms, continuously optimized its product mix, brought its core process technology to internationally advanced levels, and simultaneously pursued the development of more advanced process platforms and higher-generation products. During the reporting period, the Company's R&D expenditures were RMB 4,194,992,200, RMB 4,670,470,700, RMB 6,341,291,300, and RMB 3,660,728,000, respectively, representing a relatively high proportion of operating revenue, and amortization of intangibles arising from R&D activities and period expenses were also high. In addition, although revenue from relatively high-priced, high-margin products such as LPDDR5 and DDR5 grew rapidly during the reporting period, given the short period of mass production, the scaling-up of revenue from high-margin products requires a certain process, and the improvement in gross margins resulting from process upgrades and product optimization has yet to become further apparent.

(3) The DRAM industry exhibits strong cyclicality; during downturns, the Company's product prices fell sharply, inventory impairment losses increased substantially, and operating performance was significantly impacted.

DRAM products are highly standardized, in high demand, and subject to strong industry cycles; product prices and market scale are significantly affected by supply-demand dynamics. From the second half of 2022 through the first half of 2023, due to macro-level negative factors such as global inflation, economic recession, and geopolitical conflicts, as well as industry factors such as cyclical demand fluctuations in downstream markets, the DRAM industry experienced a historically rare and severe downturn, with market prices at their lowest declining by approximately 50% from pre-downturn levels; the three major international DRAM players also posted historically rare losses and negative gross margins in 2023. In 2023, the Company maintained revenue growth and expanded market share through a steadfast capacity expansion strategy, but its profitability was significantly impacted by the industry downturn, and inventory impairment losses and net losses increased substantially. Since 2024, as the DRAM industry has recovered, the Company's products have seen both volume and price increases; combined with continuous improvement in product mix, the Company's net losses have narrowed significantly.

2. Impact of Not Yet Achieving Profitability and the Existence of Accumulated Uncovered Losses

(1) Impact on Cash Flow

During each period of the reporting period, the net cash flows from operating activities were RMB -1,957,166,500, RMB -7,271,620,700, RMB 6,897,458,500, and RMB 4,250,519,000, respectively. The Company's operating cash flows were negative and the deficit widened in 2022 and 2023, primarily because the Company was in a phase of rapid development and implemented a strategic procurement strategy for key raw materials, resulting in relatively high cash outflows for raw material purchases and, consequently, high cash payments for goods and services received. Since 2024, the Company's operating cash flows have turned positive. As of June 30, 2025, the Company's cash and cash equivalents on its balance sheet amounted to RMB 42,922,448,900, and the Company's cash position is strong. The circumstances of not yet having achieved profitability and of having accumulated uncovered losses have not had a material adverse impact on the Company's overall funding operations or debt repayment capacity.

(2) Impact on Business Expansion

Since its establishment, the Company has built up first-generation, third-generation, and fourth-generation process technology platforms through sustained technological investment and independent innovation, and possesses an independently developed, complete technological layout in core DRAM product areas. Relying on its outstanding R&D capabilities, stable product performance, and comprehensive supporting service system, the Company's products have achieved large-scale deployment in major fields including servers, mobile phones, and computers, enabling it to accumulate an extensive base of high-quality customer relationships and to progressively establish strong brand recognition and a sound industrial ecosystem. As the Company's existing capacity is progressively released and new capacity construction is progressively completed, the Company's product competitiveness and industry influence continue to strengthen. The circumstances of not yet having achieved profitability and of having accumulated uncovered losses have not had a material adverse impact on the Company's business expansion.

(3) Impact on Talent Attraction and Team Stability

The Company places great importance on building its talent selection system and, in alignment with development needs, has formulated corresponding R&D talent strategies, utilizing various recruitment channels to attract talent and adopting flexible, market-oriented recruitment approaches to bring in high-end talent. In addition, the Company attracts and retains high-caliber R&D talent through a series of development mechanisms including training programs, mentoring and coaching, and industry exchanges, using core technical talent to nurture and lead innovative R&D teams. During the reporting period, the Company recruited widely from talent in the DRAM field; its employees have extensive industry experience and constitute a workforce capable of closing the gap with leading international players. The Company's principal senior management personnel all have many years of industry experience. The circumstances of not yet having achieved profitability and of having accumulated uncovered losses have not had a material adverse impact on team stability.

(4) Impact on R&D Investment

The Company has consistently encouraged innovation and places great emphasis on continuous technology development. During the reporting period, the Company's R&D investment remained at a substantial scale and continued to increase, with amounts for each period of the reporting period being RMB 4,194,992,200, RMB 4,670,470,700, RMB 6,341,291,300, and RMB 3,660,728,000, respectively. Under continuous substantial R&D and production investment, the Company has built the most technologically advanced and largest-scale DRAM production line in China, while keeping pace with the development of leading international manufacturers and actively positioning itself to launch more advanced generation products such as LPDDR5 and DDR5. The circumstances of not yet having achieved profitability and of having accumulated uncovered losses have not had a material adverse impact on the sustainability of the Company's R&D investment.

(5) Impact on Strategic Investment

During the reporting period, the Company strategically positioned itself in forward-looking technologies, continued to advance next-generation process improvements and diversified product development, and accelerated its efforts to catch up with internationally advanced technologies. At the same time, in order to further enhance product performance, reduce costs, and enrich its product line, the Company has been actively conducting technological exploration and strategic positioning in areas such as advanced DRAM product processes and new product applications, with the aim of strengthening the Company's overall technological capabilities, enhancing product competitiveness and market share, and achieving the goal of independent and controllable development of China's memory chip industry. The circumstances of not yet having achieved profitability and of having accumulated uncovered losses have not had a material adverse impact on the Company's strategic investment.

In summary, the Issuer is in good developmental condition. The circumstances of not yet having achieved profitability and of having accumulated uncovered losses have not produced negative effects on the Company in terms of cash flow, business expansion, talent attraction, team stability, R&D investment, strategic investment, or sustainability of production and operations, and have not constituted a material adverse impact on the Company's future ability to continue as a going concern.

3. Trend Analysis

With respect to revenue, based on the broad and continuously growing global and China market demand for DRAM, there remains significant market space for the Company's business growth. The Company's steady advancement of technology platform upgrades, diversified product development, and accelerated capacity construction and ramp-up provide important assurance for market development, and shipment volumes are expected to continue growing in 2026. In combination with the Company's product upgrade strategy and new product launch plans, the rapid scaling-up of same-generation products with larger capacities and more advanced new products such as DDR5 provides an objective basis for a certain level of improvement in overall average selling prices in 2026. Since the second half of 2025, prices for various DRAM products have shown a significant upward trend, which is favorable for the Company's revenue growth and performance improvement.

With respect to costs and expenses, the mass production of the Company's more advanced technology platforms will significantly increase per-wafer output. Combined with the Company's capacity construction and improved capacity utilization rates, economies of scale will continue to become apparent. On this basis, the Company will promote cost reduction and efficiency improvement through various operational management measures such as optimizing process pathways and increasing the adoption of domestic suppliers, with per-unit costs expected to continue declining during the forecast period. At the same time, the Company will continue to increase its R&D investment for process improvements and product development. As the Company's business scale grows rapidly, the proportions of selling expenses, general and administrative expenses, and finance costs relative to operating revenue are expected to decline.

Based on the foregoing assumptions and calculations, the Issuer expects to achieve profitability in 2026 or 2027. Under reasonable assumptions, the Company expects to achieve profitability in 2026. If 2026 falls short of expectations, as the Company's process technology platforms are upgraded, sales volumes continue to increase, and product mix continues to be optimized in 2027, profitability is expected to be achieved in 2027. The foregoing forward-looking information of the Company is a forecast based on speculative assumptions and is subject to material uncertainty; investors should exercise caution when using it in making investment decisions.

Whether the Company can achieve profitability in 2026 is primarily subject to the combined impact of average selling price and average monthly shipment volume. Applying the principle of prudence, the Company assumes that the average selling price of its products in 2026 will be lower than the actual price in September 2025. Customer payment collection has been good; the Company primarily adopts a payment-before-delivery settlement arrangement for distributors. As of the end of June 2025, the accounts receivable from Customer B's overseas entity had been fully collected.

(5) Development Expenditures

In accordance with the relevant provisions of the Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises, the Company has capitalized R&D projects that meet the capitalization criteria. During each period of the reporting period, the Company's qualifying capitalized R&D expenditures were RMB 170,860.40 thousand, RMB 15,000.00 thousand, RMB 173,400.78 thousand, and RMB 0 thousand, respectively, primarily relating to the third-generation and fourth-generation process technology platforms and related product R&D projects.

At the end of each reporting period, the carrying amount of development expenditures was RMB 0 thousand, RMB 15,000.00 thousand, RMB 0 thousand, and RMB 0 thousand, respectively, comprising primarily employee remuneration, R&D materials costs, testing service fees, and depreciation and amortization. The carrying amounts at the end of 2022 and 2024 were RMB 0 thousand, primarily because the projects involving capitalized R&D expenditures had been completed before the end of those periods, and the corresponding development expenditures were transferred to intangible assets. As of the end of June 2025, the Issuer's relevant R&D projects had not yet met the capitalization criteria.

During the reporting period, the specific circumstances under which the Issuer's R&D expenditures met the capitalization criteria are set out in the table below:

Capitalization Criteria Issuer's Actual Circumstances Satisfied?
The technical feasibility of completing the intangible asset for use or sale The completion of tape-out verification and related testing during the research phase marks the verification of the physical structural integrity and electrical functional performance of the product, and confirms that process and product design have been substantially finalized and that certain yield requirements have been met. Following assessment of technical feasibility, the Issuer's third-generation and fourth-generation process technology platforms and related product R&D projects have demonstrated a high degree of technical feasibility. Yes
The intention to complete the intangible asset and use or sell it The Issuer is committed to becoming a technically leading and commercially successful semiconductor chip memory company. During the reporting period, the Issuer was primarily engaged in the R&D of the third-generation process technology platform, the fourth-generation process technology platform, and higher-generation process technology platforms, has already achieved the production and sale of DRAM products, and will continue to R&D, produce, and sell other iterated products. The Issuer has the intention to complete the relevant intangible assets and use them. Yes
The manner in which the intangible asset will generate probable future economic benefits, including the ability to demonstrate the existence of a market for the output of the intangible asset or for the intangible asset itself or, if it is to be used internally, the usefulness of the intangible asset The R&D of the Issuer's third-generation and fourth-generation process technology platforms is conducive to further enhancing the Issuer's market competitiveness. The relevant products had broad market potential at the time of R&D. The Issuer has completed the R&D process for the relevant process technology platforms and products, and verification has confirmed that the relevant products meet market needs and can generate economic benefits in the future. Yes
The availability of adequate technical, financial, and other resources to complete the development and to use or sell the intangible asset Based on the Issuer's financial budgeting for the relevant R&D projects, technical and talent reserves, and accumulated research experience, the Issuer has sufficient resources and capabilities to complete the above project development. Yes
The ability to reliably measure the expenditure attributable to the intangible asset during its development phase The Issuer has established relevant R&D project management systems and financial accounting systems that enable effective control, aggregation, and measurement of expenditures during the development phase. Yes

During the reporting period, companies in the integrated circuit industry such as Advanced Micro-Fabrication Equipment Inc. China (中微公司), San'an Optoelectronics (三安光电), and Hygon Information Technology (海光信息) also had capitalized R&D expenditures. The relevant information is set out below:

Company Name Criteria for Distinguishing Research Phase from Development Phase
Advanced Micro-Fabrication Equipment Inc. China (中微公司) Expenditures incurred in the planned investigation, evaluation, and selection phase conducted for the purpose of researching manufacturing processes prior to the preliminary completion of prototype development are treated as research-phase expenditures and recognized in profit or loss when incurred. Expenditures incurred in the design and testing phases related to the final application of the manufacturing process from the preliminary completion of prototype development to the commencement of mass production are treated as development-phase expenditures and are capitalized.
San'an Optoelectronics (三安光电) Development activities are managed on a project basis. An "Experimental Application Form" is submitted and approved, a project budget is established, and a project number, project head, and project costs are assigned for accounting purposes. The approval of the project application serves as the demarcation point separating the research phase from the development phase, and also as the accounting demarcation point for classifying expenditures as R&D expenses or development expenditures. Upon completion of the development phase, a project completion report is prepared, and the relevant costs are transferred to intangible assets as patents or proprietary technology.
Hygon Information Technology (海光信息) At project inception, projects are broadly classified into two categories based on their nature: "technology research" and "development." "Technology research" projects relate to key technology research, and the relevant expenditures are recognized as R&D expenses when incurred. For "development" projects, a panel of review experts is convened to conduct a capitalization review, resulting in a "Capitalization Review Report." Projects that do not simultaneously satisfy all five capitalization criteria — i.e., "technology or product pre-research (technical development design and simulation verification)" — are expensed; projects in the development category that satisfy the capitalization criteria — i.e., "chip product design and implementation technology" — are capitalized.
Issuer Based on the development pathway for the Company's process technology platforms, the development cycle of each R&D project is divided into a research phase and a development phase. The research phase includes conceptualization, planning, design, and tape-out stages; the development phase includes the engineering verification stage and the customer validation stage. In accordance with the Company's accounting policy on capitalization of R&D expenses, when a project transitions from the research phase into the development phase, qualifying expenditures that meet the capitalization criteria are capitalized. The Issuer uses the receipt of an external test report on the experimental product and the completion of a comprehensive technical feasibility assessment of the technology platform by the internal team of technical experts as the point in time at which capitalization of an R&D project commences.

Source: Listed company public announcements.

Compared with companies in the integrated circuit industry such as Advanced Micro-Fabrication Equipment Inc. China (中微公司), San'an Optoelectronics (三安光电), and Hygon Information Technology (海光信息), the Company's accounting treatment for the capitalization of R&D expenditures does not differ significantly. During the reporting period, the Company has formulated relevant internal control and accounting policy documents that clearly plan and establish internal controls for each phase of R&D. The Company has established rigorous internal control systems governing R&D activities with respect to R&D planning, project initiation, process management, cost aggregation, and project review. The Company accounts for R&D expenditures on a project-by-project basis in accordance with its internal control policies, distinguishes the research phase from the development phase in accordance with project management requirements, conducts reviews as to whether the capitalization criteria are met, and capitalizes qualifying R&D expenditures during the development phase of the relevant projects. The capitalization of R&D expenditures has been executed in accordance with the Company's relevant internal control policies and has been applied consistently throughout the reporting period. At the end of each reporting period, the assets formed from capitalized development expenditures were in good condition, and no impairment provisions were recognized.

In 2023, the increase in the Company's inventory turnover ratio was primarily attributable to improvements in production capacity and output volume, the realization of economies of scale, and a decline in unit inventory costs, which led to a higher inventory turnover ratio compared to 2022.

During the reporting period, the Company's inventory turnover ratio was lower than the comparable company average, primarily because throughout the reporting period the Company was consistently in a phase of rapid capacity ramp-up rather than operating at stable and full utilization throughout the year; as production scale continued to expand, the closing balance of work-in-progress increased significantly.

The Company's operating performance recovered significantly in 2024 and the first half of 2025, primarily benefiting from the overall recovery of the DRAM market, the continuous optimization of the Company's product mix, and the positive impact of process iteration and lean production management, among other factors.

During the reporting period, the Company's current ratio and quick ratio were lower than the average of comparable companies and were close to those of SK Hynix and SMIC; the asset-to-liability ratio was higher than the average of comparable companies. The primary reasons are as follows:

  1. The Company operates in a technology- and capital-intensive industry and is currently in a phase of rapid capacity construction and ramp-up, requiring continued investment in long-term assets. Most comparable companies are currently in a mature stage with relatively stable capacity construction.
  2. The Company has not yet been listed and primarily raises funds through debt financing such as borrowings, with relatively limited financing channels, resulting in a significant amount of long-term borrowings and a higher asset-to-liability ratio. The comparable companies are all listed companies with diversified financing channels, relatively higher liquidity, and more optimized capital structures and financial conditions, which is consistent with actual conditions.

3. Analysis of Other Factors Affecting Solvency

During the reporting period, the Company maintained an overall adequate level of funds and a good credit standing with banks, with no defaults occurring. The Company's operations are normal, and the corresponding technology platform production lines will provide the Company with stable and substantial net operating cash inflows. The Company has no contingent liabilities requiring disclosure that would have a material impact on its normal production and operating activities, nor does it have any material off-balance-sheet financing arrangements. In summary, the Company faces no solvency risk.

During the reporting period, the Company's current assets were primarily composed of monetary funds. The Company accumulated substantial monetary funds through equity and debt financing. From 2022 to 2023, the Company's net cash flows from operating activities were negative, primarily because the Company was still in a rapid development stage with a relatively limited sales scale, and the cash expenditures for purchasing raw materials and other goods during the reporting period were high, resulting in significant cash outflows for purchasing goods and receiving services. In 2024, as the Company's revenue scale grew rapidly, product mix continued to improve, and lean production management was implemented, net cash flows from operating activities turned positive. As of the end of the reporting period, the Company had a monetary funds balance of RMB 4,292,244.89 million (in units of RMB '0,000), providing support for the Company's liquidity.

During the reporting period, the Company's liability structure was predominantly composed of non-current liabilities. From 2022 to 2023, the Company's asset-to-liability ratio increased, primarily because the Company supplemented large amounts of monetary funds through bank borrowings for daily operations, R&D, and production investment. From 2022 to 2024, the Company's current ratio declined year by year, primarily because the overall growth in current assets was slower than the growth in current liabilities due to rapid business development. However, the Company's short-term solvency was still relatively sound overall, with a low probability of short-term liquidity risk occurring. The Company has a good credit standing and has no overdue debts outstanding. There are no significant events or commitments affecting cash flows, and the Company's cash position is in good condition. There is no circumstance or risk trend that has or may cause a material adverse change in liquidity.

As the Company is in a phase of rapid business development with continuously growing business scale, and taking into account the characteristics of the DRAM industry, such as high R&D investment and significant cyclical market impacts, the Company is continuously improving its liquidity risk management system, further strengthening the management of sources and uses of monetary funds and effectively controlling inventory levels, so as to progressively improve cash flow levels while ensuring the steady development of the Company's business. At the same time, the Company will further accelerate the development of new products, optimize its product mix, and safeguard profitability through continuous technology upgrades, thereby enhancing its ability to withstand liquidity risk.

(七) Analysis of Going Concern Ability

During the reporting period, the Issuer operated under the IDM (Integrated Device Manufacturer) model and has been engaged in the R&D, design, production, and sales of DRAM products since its establishment, with no material change to its principal business. The Company has become China's largest and most technologically advanced DRAM enterprise, has achieved certain economies of scale, and continues to carry out technological iteration and rapid development of production capacity and scale.

During the reporting period, the Issuer had no actual controller, and its management and core technical personnel remained stable. The Issuer's assets are complete, and its business, personnel, finance, and organization are independent. During the reporting period, there were no related-party transactions that seriously affected the Issuer's independence or were manifestly unfair. As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Issuer has no material ownership disputes, material solvency risks, or material guarantee, litigation, or arbitration matters.

During the reporting period, the net profit (loss) attributable to the shareholders of the parent company was RMB -832,800.39 million, RMB -1,633,977.72 million, RMB -714,488.72 million, and RMB -233,205.82 million (in units of RMB '0,000), respectively. As of June 30, 2025, the Company has not yet achieved profitability and has accumulated unrecovered losses. Analysis of Operating Results" in this Section.

In summary, after a prudent assessment of the Company's operating conditions, the Company's management believes that, in the foreseeable future, the Company will be able to maintain good going concern ability, and there are no material adverse changes that may affect the Company's future going concern. The relevant risks relating to the Company's going concern ability are set out in "I. Risks Related to the Issuer" under "Section III: Risk Factors" of this Prospectus.

(II) Feasibility of the Fundraising Investment Projects

1. The Company's Adequate Technology Reserves and Mass Production Experience, and Its High-Quality R&D Talent Team Lay a Solid Foundation and Provide Strong Support for the Smooth Implementation of the Projects

The Company has consistently adhered to the path of independent R&D and accelerated technological innovation through generational leap R&D, completing mass production from the first-generation process technology platform to the fourth-generation process technology platform, as well as product coverage and generational evolution from DDR4 and LPDDR4X to DDR5 and LPDDR5/5X. The core products and process technologies have now reached international advanced levels. After years of development, the Company has established a comprehensive core technology system across all business segments including DRAM product design, manufacturing process, packaging and testing, and module design and application. It has also formed a comprehensive accumulation of proprietary intellectual property rights, accumulated rich experience in rapid technology iteration and industrialization, and cultivated a large number of professional and technical personnel for the DRAM memory industry. As of June 30, 2025, the Company holds a total of 3,116 domestic patents (including 2,348 invention patents) and 2,473 overseas patents, and has 4,653 R&D personnel, accounting for more than 30% of the Company's total headcount. During the reporting period, the Company's cumulative R&D investment amounted to RMB 18,867,482,300, representing 33.11% of cumulative revenue.

The technological innovation achievements accumulated over the years, the Company's continuous technological innovation capability, and the talent pool it has built are important guarantees for the implementation of this project.

2. The Continuously Growing and Evolving Downstream Application Demand Necessitates Technological Upgrades of the Company's Products

With the development of the digital economy and the continuous emergence of new technology scenarios, large models, deep learning, autonomous driving and other fields continue to iterate, the total volume of data is showing explosive growth, and the widespread demand for data reading, writing and transmission has led to an explosion in demand for high-performance memory chips in downstream application scenarios. That is, while the incremental market demand for DRAM products continues to grow, DRAM products are also required to continuously iterate toward higher storage density, lower power consumption, and faster read/write speeds. According to Omdia statistics, in 2024, within the global DRAM downstream application sectors, servers accounted for 38%, mobile devices accounted for 35%, personal computers accounted for 14%, and smart vehicles accounted for 3%, with these four major markets accounting for more than 90% of the total market. Demand for DRAM continues to grow in application areas such as servers, mobile devices, personal computers, and smart vehicles.

Therefore, with the continuous growth and evolution of downstream demand, the demand for DRAM products is becoming increasingly intense, and the Company's product technology also needs to be continuously upgraded. Through technological iteration, product competitiveness will be enhanced to continuously meet the needs of downstream products.

3. Active Supply Chain Diversification Preparations Provide Sufficient Guarantee for the Smooth Implementation of the Projects

As the leading enterprise (链主企业) in China's DRAM industry chain, based on its strong industrial influence, the Company has established close strategic cooperative relationships with upstream suppliers and downstream customers. It has actively integrated domestic industrial ecosystem resources and jointly built an interdependent industrial ecosystem, laying a solid foundation for the Company's sustained business development and active participation in global competition.

After years of development, the Company has established stable cooperative relationships with upstream suppliers including EDA vendors, materials vendors, and equipment and components vendors. Through deepening industrial chain cooperation, it has actively promoted the diversification and localization of the supply chain, enhancing supply chain security capabilities. This provides sufficient guarantee for the smooth implementation of the current fundraising investment projects and strong support for the Company's continued capacity expansion and technology process upgrading.

(III) Relationship Between the Fundraising Investment Projects and the Issuer's Existing Primary Business and Core Technologies

All fundraising investment projects under this offering are centered on the Company's existing business and core technologies. They are important vehicles for the Company to achieve technological transformation and upgrading of production lines, accelerate DRAM capacity construction, improve the industrial chain layout, and enhance technology process standards and R&D capabilities. They are consistent with the Company's future development strategy.

Section III. Issuer's Future Development Plans

(I) The Company's Strategic Plans

Chang Xin Technology (长鑫科技) upholds the mission of "empowering the information society and improving human life through memory technology." In terms of business layout, it insists on being grounded in the domestic market while gradually expanding into overseas markets. In terms of market positioning, it is accelerating the expansion of its market share in mid-to-high-end application markets. In terms of its development model, it continues to move toward coordinated industrial ecosystem development. In terms of technology R&D, it continues to deepen the development and accumulation of process technologies, continuously explores frontier technologies, and accelerates the sustained technological breakthrough of China's advanced DRAM chip industry. The Company is committed to becoming a semiconductor memory enterprise that is both technologically leading and commercially successful, and has been working tirelessly toward this goal.

(II) Measures Taken by the Issuer During the Reporting Period to Achieve Strategic Objectives and Their Implementation Results

In order to achieve the Company's overall development strategy and operational objectives, the Company specifically implemented the following measures during the reporting period:

1. Insisting on High-Level Technology R&D, Committed to Innovation-Driven Development

The Company places great importance on technological innovation and advanced process R&D. It has established a comprehensive R&D innovation system, built an experienced R&D team, and undertaken multiple national major special research projects. As of June 30, 2025, the Company has 4,653 R&D personnel, accounting for more than 30% of the Company's total headcount. During the reporting period, the Company's cumulative R&D investment amounted to RMB 18,867,482,300, representing 33.11% of cumulative revenue. In terms of technology platforms, the Company has consistently adhered to the path of independent R&D and accelerated technological innovation through generational leap R&D, completing mass production from the first-generation process technology platform to the fourth-generation process technology platform. In September 2019, the Company launched its independently designed and manufactured 8Gb DDR4 products, achieving a breakthrough "from zero to one" for the DRAM industry in mainland China. As of June 30, 2025, the development and mass production of LPDDR4X, DDR5, and LPDDR5/5X have been completed. Through continuous R&D investment and technological innovation, the Company has formed multiple scientific research achievements and core technologies. As of June 30, 2025, the Company holds a total of 3,116 domestic patents (including 2,348 invention patents) and 2,473 overseas patents.

2. Accelerating Capacity Construction and Enriching the Product Portfolio

During the reporting period, the Company continuously increased its capacity to meet downstream market demand. Based on the capacity scale as of the end of 2024 and shipment statistics for the year 2024, the Company is the number one DRAM manufacturer in China and number four in the world. In the future, as the Company's production line construction progresses steadily, the Company's overall capacity will continue to increase, and it is expected to further enhance its competitiveness in the global market.

In terms of product matrix, the Company has actively expanded its product portfolio to meet the needs of various downstream markets. It has established a diversified product layout covering the DDR series, LPDDR series, and others, and can offer diversified product solutions including memory DRAM wafers, memory DRAM chips, and memory DRAM modules. This can effectively meet the market demands of servers, mobile devices, personal computers, smart vehicles, and other markets. The Company is also actively deploying and planning to launch products of newer generations to continuously meet the rapidly growing needs of the next-generation information technology industry.

3. Promoting Supply Chain Development and Driving the Joint Development of Upstream and Downstream Enterprises

During the reporting period, the Company took a series of measures surrounding supply chain development, fully leveraging the resource advantages of leading enterprises and promoting broad participation from scientific research institutes, universities, and key enterprises in the upstream and downstream of the industry. The Company focused on the development and verification of the supply chain, effectively driving the coordinated development of core segments of related upstream and downstream industrial chains such as semiconductor equipment and semiconductor materials.

4. Attracting, Cultivating, and Retaining Key Talent, and Strengthening Constraint and Incentive Mechanisms

Talent is the foundation for the sustained development of integrated circuit enterprises, and key talent is particularly important for the DRAM industry, which must continuously pursue advanced processes. On one hand, the Company continuously optimizes its compensation system and incentive structure, constantly stimulating the innovative drive of its core talent pool and deeply binding the Company's future development with key talent. On the other hand, the Company continuously increases its investment in cultivating key talent, builds clear career development pathways for them, and achieves a win-win outcome for talent growth and Company development through multiple approaches such as building high-quality internal faculty teams, developing various training and curriculum systems, and cooperating with major domestic universities.

(III) The Issuer's Specific Future Development Plans and Measures to Be Taken

In the future, the Company will continue to adhere to its current strategic plans, insist on high-level technology R&D, continuously deepen the development and accumulation of process technologies, continuously explore frontier technologies, and consolidate its core technology foundation. It will accelerate the deployment and construction of capacity, expand its diversified product portfolio, and actively explore and deploy in mid-to-high-end niche markets and overseas markets. It will enhance team building, strengthen operational capabilities, increase talent density, and continuously improve the Company's market position and competitive advantages. At the same time, the Company will actively expand its financing channels to better support future strategic development.

In the arrangement for the use of fundraising proceeds under this offering, in conjunction with future development plans, the Company intends to invest the fundraising proceeds into the Memory Wafer Manufacturing Mass Production Line Technology Upgrade and Transformation Project, the DRAM Memory Technology Upgrade Project, and the Dynamic Random Access Memory Forward-Looking Technology Research and Development Project. After the implementation of these projects, they will be able to meet the Company's needs to further enhance its technology and R&D capabilities and accelerate capacity construction and upgrading in the DRAM industry. They are important measures for the Company to continuously deepen the development and accumulation of process technologies, continuously explore frontier technologies, accelerate capacity construction, and enhance core competitiveness, and are conducive to the realization of the Company's development goal of becoming a technologically leading and commercially successful semiconductor memory enterprise.

V. The Issuer's Ability to Operate Independently and Sustainably Directly Facing the Market

Since its establishment, the Company has operated in a standardized manner in strict accordance with the requirements of the Company Law, the Securities Law, the Articles of Association, and other laws, regulations, and rules. In terms of assets, personnel, finance, institutions, and business, the Company is independent, has a complete business system and the ability to operate independently and sustainably directly facing the market, and possesses a complete R&D, procurement, production, and sales system.

(I) Complete Assets

The Company was established through the overall conversion of Ruili Integrated (睿力集成) into a joint-stock company, inheriting all assets, liabilities, and equity of the original limited liability company. The Company possesses complete qualifications, assets, and supporting facilities required for its business operations, legally holds ownership or use rights over land, properties, equipment, trademarks, patents, and other assets required for operations, and has an independent raw material procurement and product sales system. The Company's assets are complete, with a business system and related assets relating to its operations, and there is no situation in which assets or funds have been occupied by major shareholders in a manner that damages the Company's interests.

(II) Personnel Independence

The Company has a completely independent labor, human resources, and wage management system, as well as an independent employee workforce. The Company has established a sound corporate governance structure, and directors, audit committee members, and senior management are elected and appointed strictly in accordance with the Articles of Association and other relevant regulations. There is no situation in which the Company's senior management holds positions other than director or supervisor at the Company's major shareholders or enterprises controlled by them and receives remuneration therefrom, nor is there any situation in which financial personnel hold concurrent positions at the Company's major shareholders or enterprises controlled by them.

(III) Financial Independence

The Company has established an independent financial accounting department, staffed with dedicated financial personnel, established an independent and complete accounting system, and formulated internal financial management systems, enabling it to make financial decisions independently. The Company has independent bank accounts and fulfills independent tax obligations as an independent taxpayer.

(IV) Institutional Independence

In accordance with the requirements of the Company Law and the Articles of Association, the shareholders' general meeting serves as the supreme authority, the board of directors serves as the decision-making body, and the audit committee serves as the supervisory body, establishing a complete corporate governance structure. The Company has established sound internal organizational structures suited to its own development needs, established corresponding internal management systems, has independent functional departments that independently exercise operational and management authority, and there are no instances of functional institutions being commingled with major shareholders and enterprises controlled by them in terms of business premises, office premises, management systems, and other aspects.

(V) Business Independence

The Issuer has no controlling shareholder or actual controller. There are no unfair competition or obviously inequitable related-party transactions between the Company and its major shareholders and other enterprises controlled by them. There are no situations of interest transfer through customers or suppliers, or mutual or unilateral concession of business opportunities. The Company has a complete and independent operational management system for R&D, procurement, production, and sales, and the Company's business is independent from its major shareholders and other enterprises controlled by them.

(VI) Stability of Primary Business, Management Team, and Core Technical Personnel

As the largest company in China's domestic DRAM industry to have achieved independent R&D design and commercial mass production, the Company has leading technology levels and industrialization capabilities in China's DRAM field. The Company's primary business is stable, with no changes in primary business in the most recent two years.

The Company's management team and core technical personnel are stable. In the most recent two years, there have been no material adverse changes among directors, supervisors, senior management, and core technical personnel.

(VII) No Material Events Affecting Independent Sustainable Operations

During the reporting period, the Company had no material ownership disputes over primary assets, core technologies, or trademarks; no material debt repayment risks; no material contingent liabilities such as significant guarantees, litigation, or arbitration; and no other matters such as material changes that have occurred or will occur in the operating environment that would have a material adverse impact on the Company's independent sustainable operations.

VI. Competition in the Same Industry

(I) Explanation of Whether Competition in the Same Industry Exists

The Issuer has no controlling shareholder or actual controller, and there are no situations where other enterprises controlled by the actual controller and its persons acting in concert compete with the Company in the same industry. As of the signing date of this Prospectus, there is no competition in the same industry between the Issuer and the major shareholders holding 5% or more of the Company's shares and other enterprises controlled by them.

(II) Undertakings Regarding Avoidance of Competition in the Same Industry and Maintenance of Business Independence

Qinghui Jidian (清辉集电), the Company's largest shareholder, has issued a Letter of Undertaking Regarding Avoidance of Competition in the Same Industry. For details, please refer to "Section XII Appendices," "V. Undertakings Related to Investor Protection," "(V) Undertaking of the Largest Shareholder Qinghui Jidian Regarding Avoidance of Competition in the Same Industry" of this Prospectus.

(1) GigaDevice Semiconductor Group (兆易创新集团)

During the reporting period, the content of the Issuer's sales to GigaDevice Semiconductor Group primarily included its own DRAM products and the provision of foundry services. The specific situations are as follows:

1) Provision of Foundry Services

GigaDevice Semiconductor Group is a semiconductor design company adopting the Fabless model. The Company is one of the few domestic IDM manufacturers in China with the capability to provide foundry services for DRAM products. In order to achieve product deployment and sales in the DRAM niche market, GigaDevice Semiconductor Group has successively purchased wafer foundry services for DRAM products from the Company. As the contract manufacturing products were mass-produced, the Company's foundry service revenue from GigaDevice Semiconductor Group increased during the reporting period.

2) Sales of DRAM Products

During the reporting period, on one hand, GigaDevice Semiconductor Group purchased DRAM chips or wafers from the Company based on its own product development and customer service needs. On the other hand, in the early stages of market development, the Company sold DRAM products through GigaDevice Semiconductor Group as a distributor. As the Company's business scale expanded and its sales channels were improved, the Company ceased selling DRAM products through GigaDevice Semiconductor Group as a distributor in the second half of 2023.

During the reporting period, the pricing of the Company's cooperation with GigaDevice Semiconductor Group was negotiated based on market prices, and is fair. With the expansion of the Company's revenue scale, the proportion of related-party transactions has declined year by year, and this has not had a material adverse impact on the Issuer's financial condition, operating results, or primary business.

(2) Customer O (客户 O)

During the reporting period, the Issuer primarily provided Customer O with comprehensive technical services including tape-out (流片), sample analysis, and testing services. Customer O is a privately-run non-enterprise unit (民办非企业单位) that primarily conducts academic research, academic exchanges, professional training, achievement transformation, technical R&D, technical consulting, technical promotion and application related to memory. The Issuer is the largest, most technologically advanced, and most comprehensively deployed integrated DRAM R&D, design, and manufacturing enterprise in China, and both parties have a natural basis for cooperation in the memory field. The prices agreed upon by both parties were determined through negotiation and are fair, and this has not had a material adverse impact on the Issuer's financial

(VIII) Institutional Arrangements for Regulating Related-Party Transactions

The Company has established corresponding institutional safeguards for regulating related-party transactions. In accordance with the Company Law and other laws and regulations, the Company has established a well-regulated and sound corporate governance structure, elected independent directors, and formulated the Independent Directors Work System to ensure the independence of the Board of Directors and to improve the corporate governance structure. To ensure that related-party transactions are open, fair, and impartial, the Company has, in accordance with the Company Law, Guidelines for the Articles of Association of Listed Companies, and other relevant laws, regulations, and normative documents, formulated the Articles of Association, Rules of Procedure for Shareholders' Meetings, Rules of Procedure for the Board of Directors, Independent Directors Work System, and Administrative Measures for Related-Party Transactions, among other rules and regulations, which set out detailed provisions on the decision-making authority and procedures for related-party transactions.

(IX) Measures to Regulate and Reduce Related-Party Transactions

For transactions conducted with related parties based on business needs in the course of daily production and operations, the Issuer will, in accordance with relevant laws and regulations and rules such as the Articles of Association, Rules of Procedure for Shareholders' Meetings, Rules of Procedure for the Board of Directors, Independent Directors Work System, and Administrative Measures for Related-Party Transactions, strictly comply with the basic principles, decision-making procedures, recusal system, and information disclosure requirements applicable to related-party transactions, and give full play to the role of independent directors in practice to ensure the fairness of related-party transaction prices and the compliance of approval procedures, thereby protecting the interests of other shareholders of the Issuer. Shareholders holding 5% or more of the Issuer's shares and their persons acting in concert, directors, and senior management have issued a Letter of Commitment on Regulating Related-Party Transactions. For the specific content of such commitments, please refer to the section of this Prospectus entitled "Section 12 Appendices," subsection "VI. Other Commitment Matters Made by the Issuer and Other Responsible Parties in Connection with This Offering and Listing," item "(I) Commitments on Regulating Related-Party Transactions."

(X) Performance of Decision-Making Procedures for Related-Party Transactions and Opinions of Independent Directors

The meetings confirmed that the related-party transactions occurring during the most recent three years and one period complied with the principles of openness, fairness, and impartiality, and that there were no circumstances of damage to the Company's or other shareholders' legitimate rights and interests, no circumstances of inauthentic transactions or unfair pricing, and no circumstances affecting the Company's independence.

Section 9 Investor Protection

I. Distribution Arrangements for Retained Earnings Prior to Completion of This Offering and Decision-Making Procedures Already Completed

On September 25, 2025, the Company convened its Third Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders of 2025, which reviewed and approved the Proposal on the Disposal Plan for Retained Undistributed Profits Prior to the Company's Initial Public Offering. Prior to the listing of this offering, the Company may distribute profits pursuant to a resolution of the shareholders' meeting. The retained undistributed profits (accumulated unrecovered losses) shall be shared (borne) in their entirety by both new and existing shareholders after the listing in proportion to their respective shareholdings following the listing.

II. Issuer's Dividend Distribution Policy

(I) Dividend Distribution Policy Following This Offering

On September 25, 2025, the Company convened its Third Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders of 2025, which reviewed and approved the Proposal on the Three-Year Dividend Return Plan for Shareholders Following the Company's Initial Public Offering and Listing on the STAR Market. The meeting adopted corresponding provisions on the dividend distribution policy following this offering and listing, as set out below:

1. Principles of Profit Distribution

The Company implements a stable and sustainable profit distribution policy. The Company attaches importance to providing reasonable investment returns to investors, while giving consideration to the Company's long-term interests and sustainable development, and maintaining the continuity and stability of profit distributions. During the decision-making and deliberation process, the opinions of independent directors and minority shareholders shall be fully solicited and considered. Profit distributions by the Company shall not exceed the scope of accumulated distributable profits and shall not impair the Company's ability to continue as a going concern.

2. Forms of Profit Distribution

The Company may distribute profits in the form of cash dividends, stock dividends, a combination of both, or such other methods as permitted by laws and regulations. However, when selecting the method of profit distribution, the Company shall prioritize distribution in the form of cash dividends. Where the Company meets the conditions for cash dividends, it shall distribute profits by way of cash dividends. Where stock dividends are used for profit distribution, genuine and reasonable factors such as the Company's growth potential and the dilution of net assets per share shall be fully considered.

3. Conditions and Proportions for Dividend Distribution

Dividends shall be distributed when the following conditions are met:

(1) On the premise that the Company achieves a profit in the current year, has a positive cumulative undistributed profit balance, and is able to continue operating and developing in the long term, if the Company has no major capital expenditure plans, the Company shall prioritize distributing profits in the form of cash dividends. When the conditions for cash dividends are met, the profits distributed by the Company each year in the form of cash shall be no less than 10% of the distributable profits realized in that year, and the cumulative profits distributed in cash over the most recent three years shall be no less than 30% of the average annual distributable profits realized over the most recent three years. The specific dividend ratio for each year shall be proposed by the Board of Directors based on the Company's annual profitability and future capital usage plans.

(2) When the Company's operations are in good condition and the Board of Directors believes that the Company's earnings per share and share price are not commensurate with its share capital size and capital structure, the Company may, on the premise of meeting the above cash dividend ratio requirements, also distribute profits by way of stock dividends.

(3) The Board of Directors of the Company shall comprehensively consider factors such as the characteristics of the industry in which it operates, its stage of development, its own business model, profitability, debt repayment capacity, whether there are major capital expenditure plans, and investor returns, and distinguish among the following circumstances. In accordance with the procedures stipulated in the Articles of Association applicable after the Company's listing, the Board shall propose differentiated cash dividend policies:

  1. If the Company is in the mature stage of development and has no major capital expenditure plans, when distributing profits, the proportion of cash dividends in the current profit distribution shall be no less than 80%;
  2. If the Company is in the mature stage of development and has major capital expenditure plans, when distributing profits, the proportion of cash dividends in the current profit distribution shall be no less than 40%;
  3. If the Company is in the growth stage of development and has major capital expenditure plans, when distributing profits, the proportion of cash dividends in the current profit distribution shall be no less than 20%.

When making actual dividend distributions, the Company's Board of Directors shall determine the specific arrangements based on the actual development stage and specific circumstances. Where it is not easy to distinguish the Company's stage of development but there are major capital expenditure plans, the provisions of item 3) of this paragraph may be applied.

The proportion of cash dividends in the current profit distribution equals cash dividends divided by the sum of cash dividends and stock dividends.

The Company's dividend distributions shall not exceed the scope of accumulated distributable profits.

4. Specific Conditions for Cash Dividends

For the Company to distribute cash dividends, all of the following conditions must be met simultaneously:

  1. The Company achieves a profit in that year and the distributable profit realized (i.e., the after-tax profit remaining after the Company makes up for losses and draws reserves) is positive, and after the cash dividend distribution, the Company's cash flow can still meet the Company's needs for continued operation and long-term development;
  2. The auditing firm issues a standard unqualified audit opinion on the Company's financial report for that year;
  3. No special circumstances such as major capital expenditure plans are expected to arise within the next twelve months (excluding the investment projects funded by the proceeds of this offering and listing).

In circumstances where the Company does not meet condition 3) above for cash dividends but decides to proceed with a cash dividend distribution, the differentiated cash dividend policy set out in item (3) of Article 3 above shall be followed.

5. Interval Between Cash Dividend Distributions

When the conditions for dividend distribution are met, the Company shall in principle distribute cash dividends once per fiscal year. The Board of Directors of the Company may, based on the scale of current earnings, cash flow status, stage of development, and capital requirements, propose that the Company conduct an interim dividend distribution.

6. Conditions for Stock Dividend Distribution

When the Company's operations are in good condition and the Board of Directors believes that the distribution of stock dividends is in the overall interest of all shareholders of the Company, the Company may, on the premise of ensuring adequate cash dividend distributions, propose a stock dividend distribution plan. Stock dividend distributions shall be supported by genuine and reasonable factors such as the Company's growth potential and the dilution of net assets per share.

7. Decision-Making Procedures and Mechanisms for Profit Distribution

When distributing profits, the Board of Directors of the Company shall first formulate and deliberate on a distribution plan in accordance with the Articles of Association, taking into account the Company's profitability, capital requirements, and shareholder return plan.

When deliberating on a specific cash dividend plan, the Board of Directors shall carefully study and deliberate on matters such as the timing and conditions for cash dividends, the minimum proportion, the conditions for adjustment, and the procedural requirements for decision-making. The Board shall also solicit the opinions of independent directors in writing in advance, and the independent directors shall express a clear opinion. Where an independent director believes that the specific cash dividend plan may harm the interests of the Company or minority shareholders, the independent director has the right to express an independent opinion. If the Board of Directors does not adopt or does not fully adopt the opinions of the independent directors, the independent directors' opinions and the specific reasons for non-adoption shall be recorded in the board resolution and disclosed. Independent directors may solicit the opinions of minority shareholders, propose dividend proposals, and submit them directly to the Board of Directors for deliberation. After the Board of Directors reviews and passes the profit distribution plan, it shall be submitted to the shareholders' meeting for review and approval.

The Audit Committee shall monitor the Board of Directors' implementation of the cash dividend policy and shareholder return plan, as well as its compliance with the corresponding decision-making procedures and information disclosure requirements. If the Audit Committee finds that the Board of Directors has failed to strictly implement the cash dividend policy and shareholder return plan, has failed to strictly comply with the corresponding decision-making procedures, or has failed to make accurate, truthful, and complete information disclosures, it shall urge the Board to make timely corrections.

When the shareholders' meeting deliberates on a profit distribution plan, the Company shall provide shareholders with an online voting option, and shall proactively communicate and interact with shareholders, particularly minority shareholders, through various channels to fully solicit the opinions and demands of minority shareholders and to respond promptly to issues of concern to minority shareholders.

If the Company achieves a profit in the current year and meets the conditions for cash dividends, but the Board of Directors has not submitted a profit distribution proposal to the shareholders' meeting in accordance with the established profit distribution policy, the Board shall explain the reasons in its periodic report, along with the intended use and deployment plan for the funds not used for dividend distribution, and the independent directors shall issue an independent opinion.

8. Amendments to the Profit Distribution Policy

If adjustments to the profit distribution policy are required due to significant changes in the external operating environment or the Company's own operating conditions, such adjustments shall take the protection of shareholders' equity as the starting point. If the Company determines that it is genuinely necessary to adjust or change the profit distribution policy, the amended profit distribution policy shall be submitted to the shareholders' meeting for review and approval.

Significant changes in the Company's external operating environment or its own operating conditions refer to any of the following circumstances:

  1. Significant changes in national laws, regulations, or industry policies that have a material adverse impact on the Company's production and operations, resulting in operating losses;
  2. Force majeure events such as war, natural disasters, or major public health incidents that have a material adverse impact on the Company's production and operations, resulting in operating losses;
  3. Due to significant changes in the external operating environment or the Company's own operating conditions, the ratio of net cash flows from operating activities to net profit for each of three consecutive fiscal years is below 20%;
  4. Other matters stipulated by the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) and securities exchanges.

9. Deduction of Cash Dividends in Cases of Illegal Occupation of Company Funds by Shareholders

Where a shareholder illegally occupies the Company's funds, the Company shall deduct the cash dividends allocated to that shareholder to repay the funds so occupied.

10. Cycle and Adjustment Mechanism for Shareholder Dividend Return Plans

(1) The Company shall formulate a shareholder return plan on a three-year cycle. On the basis of summarizing the implementation of the previous three-year shareholder return plan, the Company shall, having fully considered the various factors facing the Company as well as the opinions of shareholders (especially minority public shareholders), independent directors, and the Audit Committee, determine whether it is necessary to adjust the Company's profit distribution policy and the shareholder return plan for the next three years.

(2) In the event of force majeure such as war, natural disasters, or major public health incidents, or significant changes in the Company's external operating environment that have a material impact on the Company's production and operations, or significant changes in the Company's own operating conditions, or where the current specific shareholder return plan affects the Company's ability to continue as a going concern, and it is genuinely necessary to adjust the shareholder return plan, the Company may reformulate the shareholder return plan in accordance with the basic principles of profit distribution established under this article.

(II) Differences Between Dividend Distribution Policies Before and After This Offering

The main difference between the dividend distribution policies before and after this offering lies in the further refinement of the profit distribution policy following the offering, with clear provisions on distribution principles, conditions and proportions for dividend distribution, decision-making procedures and mechanisms for profit distribution plans, and amendments to the profit distribution policy.

III. Special Voting Rights Shares, Contractual Control Structures or Similar Special Arrangements, Circumstances of Not Yet Achieving Profitability or Accumulated Unrecovered Losses, and Investor Protection Measures

Pursuant to the Articles of Association (Draft), shareholders shall enjoy rights and bear obligations in accordance with the class of shares held. Shareholders holding the same class of shares shall enjoy equal rights and bear the same obligations. The Articles of Association (Draft) does not establish special voting rights shares for any specific shareholder, and there are no special contractual control arrangements among the Company's shareholders.

As of the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Company has not yet achieved sustained profitability and has accumulated unrecovered losses. Major shareholders and other relevant parties of the Company have made commitments regarding share lock-up and share reduction arrangements. For details, please refer to the section of this Prospectus entitled "Section 12 Appendices," subsection "V. Commitments Related to Investor Protection," item "(I) Commitments on Lock-Up Arrangements for Shares Held by Pre-Offering Shareholders, Voluntary Share Lock-Up, Extension of Lock-Up Periods, and Shareholders' Shareholding and Reduction Intentions."

四、落实投资者关系管理相关规定的安排、股利分配决策程序、股东投票机制建立情况

（一）落实投资者关系管理相关规定的安排

为切实保护投资者特别是中小投资者的合法权益、完善公司治理结构，公司根据《公司法》《证券法》等法律法规的规定，建立了完善的投资者权益保护制度并严格执行，真实、准确、完整、及时地报送和披露信息，积极合理地实施利润分配政策，保证投资者依法获取公司信息、享有资产收益、参与重大决策和选择管理者等方面的权利。

1、信息披露制度和流程

为规范公司信息披露行为，确保信息披露真实、准确、完整、及时，切实保护公司、股东及投资者的合法权益，发行人根据《公司法》《证券法》《上市公司信息披露管理办法》《上市规则》等相关法律法规及《公司章程》的有关规定，制定了《信息披露管理制度》，明确了公司管理人员在信息披露中的责任和义务。该制度有助于加强公司与投资者之间的信息沟通，提升规范运作和公司治理水平，为投资者尤其是中小投资者提供了制度保障。

本次发行上市后，发行人将严格执行上述制度与办法，保障投资者的知情权、决策参与权，切实保护投资者的合法权益。

2、投资者沟通渠道建立情况

公司设置了董事会办公室负责具体信息披露和投资者关系管理工作，指定董事会秘书担任投资者关系管理负责人，并配备专门工作人员，负责开展投资者关系管理工作，管理、运行和维护投资者关系管理的相关渠道和平台。董事会秘书将负责接待投资者来访，回答投资者咨询，向投资者提供信息披露资料等相关工作。

3、未来开展投资者关系管理的规划

为加强投资者关系管理，提高信息披露质量，促进投资者对公司的了解，发行人将依照《投资者关系管理制度》切实开展投资者关系构建、管理和维护的相关工作，积极听取投资者的意见与建议，为投资者和公司搭建起畅通的沟通交流平台，保障投资者依法享有获取公司信息、享有资产收益、参与重大决策与选择管理者的相关权利，切实维护全体股东，尤其是中小股东的利益，努力实现公司价值最大化和股东利益最大化。

1-1-313

（二）股利分配决策程序

公司发行上市后的股利分配决策程序参见本招股说明书"第九节 投资者保护"之"二、发行人股利分配政策"。

（三）股东投票机制建立情况

公司《公司章程》《股东会议事规则》对股东投票机制作出了明确规定，包括累积投票制度选举董事、中小投资者单独计票机制、网络投票方式召开股东会进行审议表决、征集投票权机制的相关安排等，具体内容如下：

1、累积投票机制

《公司章程》规定，公司股东会就选举董事进行表决时，根据公司章程的规定或者股东会的决议，可以实行累积投票制。公司单一股东及其一致行动人拥有权益的股份比例在百分之三十以上的，或者股东会选举两名以上独立董事的，应当采用累积投票制。

前款所称累积投票制是指股东会选举董事时，每一股份拥有与应选董事人数相同的表决权，股东拥有的表决权可以集中使用。董事会应当向股东公布候选董事的简历和基本情况。

股东会以累积投票方式选举董事的，独立董事和非独立董事的表决应当分别进行。

2、中小投资者单独计票机制

股东会审议影响中小投资者利益的重大事项时，对中小投资者表决应当单独计票。单独计票结果应当及时公开披露。

3、网络投票机制

公司召开股东会的地点为：公司住所地或日常办公地或股东会通知中载明的地点。现场会议时间、地点的选择应当便于股东参加。

股东会将设置会场，以现场会议形式召开。股东会除设置会场以现场形式召开外，还可以同时采用电子通信方式召开。公司还将提供通讯表决、网络投票等方式为股东参加股东会提供便利。股东通过上述方式参加股东会的，视为出席。

4、征集投票权机制

公司董事会、独立董事、持有百分之一以上有表决权股份的股东或者依照法律、行

1-1-314

investor protection institutions established in accordance with laws, administrative regulations, or the provisions of the China Securities Regulatory Commission may publicly solicit shareholder voting rights. When soliciting shareholder voting rights, sufficient disclosure of specific voting intentions and other information shall be made to the solicited parties. It is prohibited to solicit shareholder voting rights in a paid or disguised paid manner. Except as required by law, the Company may not impose a minimum shareholding ratio restriction on the solicitation of voting rights.

V. Commitments Related to Investor Protection

(I) Commitments Regarding Lock-up Arrangements for Shares Held by Pre-IPO Shareholders, Voluntary Share Lock-ups, Extensions of Lock-up Periods, and Shareholders' Intentions Regarding Shareholding and Reduction of Holdings

1. Commitments Regarding Lock-up Arrangements for Shares Held, Voluntary Share Lock-ups, and Extensions of Lock-up Periods

(1) Commitments of Shareholders Holding in Aggregate No Less Than 51% of the Total Shares Prior to the Offering

1) Commitment of Qinghui Jidian (清辉集电)

"1. This enterprise shall strictly fulfill the share lock-up commitments disclosed in the Issuer's initial public offering prospectus. Within 36 months from the date on which the Issuer's shares are listed on the stock exchange, the Covenantor shall not transfer or entrust others to manage the shares of the Issuer already issued prior to the initial public offering held by the Covenantor, nor shall the Issuer repurchase the aforementioned shares held by the Covenantor.

2. Prior to the Issuer achieving profitability, within 3 complete fiscal years from the date on which the Issuer's shares commence trading on the stock exchange, this enterprise shall not transfer or entrust others to manage the shares of the Issuer directly or indirectly held by this enterprise prior to this offering and listing, nor shall it propose that the Issuer repurchase such shares; during the 4th and 5th fiscal years from the date on which the Issuer's shares commence trading on the stock exchange, the annual reduction of shares of the Issuer directly or indirectly held by this enterprise prior to this offering and listing shall not exceed 2% of the Issuer's total share capital. After the Issuer achieves profitability, this enterprise may reduce its holdings of the Issuer's shares directly or indirectly held prior to this offering and listing starting from the day following the disclosure of the Issuer's annual report for that year, provided that it shall comply with other provisions of this commitment letter.

3. If the Issuer's net profit in the year of listing (measured by net profit attributable to the parent company after deducting non-recurring gains and losses, the same below) declines by 50% or more compared to the year prior to listing, the lock-up period for shares held by this enterprise at that time shall be extended by 12 months; if the Issuer's net profit in the second year after listing declines by 50% or more compared to the year prior to listing, the lock-up period for shares held by this enterprise at that time shall be further extended by an additional 12 months on the basis of the preceding item; if the Issuer's net profit in the third year after listing declines by 50% or more compared to the year prior to listing, the lock-up period for shares held by this enterprise at that time shall be further extended by an additional 12 months on the basis of the preceding two items. The aforementioned "shares held at that time" refer to shares acquired by this enterprise before the Issuer's listing that are still held by this enterprise at the time of the Issuer's annual report disclosure in the year of listing and in the second and third years thereafter, respectively.

4. If, within 6 months after the Issuer's shares are listed, the closing price of the Issuer's shares is below the initial public offering price for 20 consecutive trading days, or the closing price at the end of the 6-month period after listing (if such day is not a trading day, then the first trading day following such day) is below the initial public offering price, the lock-up period for the shares of the Issuer held by this enterprise prior to the initial public offering shall automatically be extended by at least 6 months.

5. The Covenantor shall strictly comply with the relevant provisions of laws, regulations, normative documents, and the business rules of the stock exchange on which the Company is listed regarding shareholders' shareholding and share changes (including reductions in holdings).

6. During the period in which the Covenantor holds shares, if the laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities applicable to share lock-up and reduction of holdings change, the Covenantor shall apply the amended laws, regulations, normative documents, policies, and requirements of securities regulatory authorities in accordance with the requirements of the relevant laws and regulations."
