1. Undertakings of the Issuer

"The Company will fulfill all obligations in accordance with the relevant provisions of the Proposal Regarding the Pre-Plan for Stabilizing the Share Price Within Three Years Following the Company's Initial Public Offering and Listing on the STAR Market (hereinafter referred to as the 'Share Price Stabilization Proposal').

The Company undertakes and guarantees that newly appointed directors and senior management members in the future will fulfill the corresponding undertaking requirements made by directors and senior management members at the time of the Company's offering and listing, as well as the corresponding requirements of the Company's Share Price Stabilization Proposal."

2. Undertakings of the Issuer's Directors and Senior Management

Directors and senior management members of the Issuer, namely Zhu Yiming (朱一明), Cao Kanyu (曹堪宇), Xie Shumin (谢树民), Zhao Lun (赵纶), Zhang Yu (张羽), Zhu Wenju (朱文菊), Huang Danyang (黄丹阳), Feng Pengxi (冯鹏熙), Yuan Yuan (袁园), and Li Hongwen (李红文), undertake as follows:

"I will, in accordance with the relevant provisions of the Proposal Regarding the Pre-Plan for Stabilizing the Share Price Within Three Years Following the Company's Initial Public Offering and Listing on the STAR Market (hereinafter referred to as the 'Share Price Stabilization Proposal'), vote in favor of resolutions regarding share repurchase proposals at board meetings convened by the Company for share repurchase matters (if voting rights are applicable); and fulfill all relevant obligations in accordance with the relevant provisions of the Share Price Stabilization Proposal."

(V) Undertaking by the Largest Shareholder Qinghui Jidian (清辉集电) Regarding the Prevention of Competition in the Same Industry

The Issuer's largest shareholder, Qinghui Jidian (清辉集电), undertakes as follows:

1-1-331

"1. As of the date of this letter of undertaking, the undersigned and subsidiaries directly or indirectly controlled by the undersigned have not, in any manner, directly or indirectly engaged in any business that constitutes competition or potential competition in the same industry with the principal business of the Issuer or its subsidiaries, including but not limited to not independently or jointly, on behalf of any person, firm, or company (entity, enterprise), developing, operating or assisting in operating, participating in, or engaging in such related business.

2. From the date of this letter of undertaking, the undersigned undertakes that it will not: (1) newly engage, independently or with a third party, in the form of direct or indirect control, in any business or activities that constitute competition or potential competition in the same industry having a material adverse impact on the principal business of the Issuer or its subsidiaries (hereinafter referred to as 'Competing Business'); (2) if the undersigned and subsidiaries directly or indirectly controlled by the undersigned obtain new business opportunities to acquire, in any manner, controlling shares, equity interests, or interests in entities competing with the principal business of the Issuer and its subsidiaries, the undersigned will notify the Issuer in writing, and if the Issuer provides a written response within the reasonable period specified in the notice indicating its willingness to accept such new investment opportunity, the undersigned or subsidiaries directly or indirectly controlled by the undersigned (excluding the Issuer and its subsidiaries) shall, within the legal framework, endeavor to cause such new business opportunity to be offered first to the Issuer or its subsidiaries on reasonable and fair terms and conditions.

3. This letter of undertaking shall become effective from the date of issuance and shall terminate upon the occurrence of any of the following: (1) the undersigned/and persons acting in concert (if any) are no longer the largest shareholder of the Issuer; (2) the shares of the Issuer cease to be listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange (excluding any suspension of trading in the Issuer's shares for any reason); (3) when national regulations no longer require certain undertaking content, the corresponding portion shall automatically terminate.

4. 'Subsidiary' with respect to any party to this letter of undertaking means any other entity or unit (whether or not having legal person status) that is (1) held or controlled by it with 50% or more of the issued share capital or enjoys 50% or more of the voting rights (if applicable), or (2) entitled to enjoy 50% or more of the after-tax profits, or (3) entitled to control the composition of the board of directors or otherwise control such other entity or unit, as well as the subsidiaries of such other entity or unit."

(I) Undertakings Regarding the Regulation of Related-Party Transactions

Shareholders holding 5% or more of the Company's shares and persons acting in concert, as well as directors and senior management, have issued a Letter of Undertaking Regarding the Regulation of Related-Party Transactions, with specific contents as follows:

1. Undertakings of Shareholders Holding 5% or More of the Issuer's Shares and Persons Acting in Concert: Qinghui Jidian (清辉集电), Changxin Integrated (长鑫集成), NFIC Phase II (大基金二期), Hefei Jixin (合肥集鑫), Anhui Provincial Investment (安徽省投), and Industrial Investment High Growth (产投高成长)

"1. The undersigned and other enterprises controlled by the undersigned shall not exploit the undersigned's position as a shareholder holding 5% or more of the Issuer's shares to occupy the funds of the Issuer and its subsidiaries. The undersigned and other enterprises controlled by the undersigned shall regulate related-party transactions with the Issuer and its subsidiaries. Any unavoidable business dealings or transactions shall be conducted on the principles of fairness, reasonableness, and equal compensation; the transaction price shall be determined at a reasonable price recognized by the market; relevant agreements shall be signed; and information disclosure obligations shall be fulfilled in accordance with applicable regulations.

2. The undersigned guarantees that the undersigned and other enterprises controlled by the undersigned, as well as nominated directors, will strictly comply with the abstention procedures during the voting on related-party transaction matters at the Issuer's board of directors' and shareholders' meetings when deliberating on related-party transaction matters involving the Issuer, in accordance with laws, regulations, normative documents, and the Issuer's articles of association.

3. The undersigned guarantees that the undersigned, other enterprises controlled by the undersigned, and nominated directors will strictly comply with the Issuer's decision-making system for related-party transactions, and will ensure that the legitimate interests of the Issuer and other shareholders are not harmed; and guarantees that the undersigned's position and influence in the Issuer will not be used to harm the legitimate rights and interests of the Issuer and other shareholders through related-party transactions."

members of the Reorganization Committee, members of the Advisory Committee, or other persons with an interest relationship, in the following manners:

VII. Establishment, Improvement, and Operation of the Shareholders' Meeting, Board of Directors, Audit Committee, Independent Directors, and Board Secretary Systems

In accordance with the relevant provisions of laws and regulations including the Company Law and the Securities Law, as well as the Articles of Association, the Company has established and improved the shareholders' meeting, board of directors, audit committee, independent directors, board secretary, and other systems, forming an operational mechanism in which the authority body, decision-making body, supervisory body, and executive body coordinate with and check and balance each other.

To further improve the corporate governance structure, the Company has established under the Board of Directors a Strategy Committee, an Audit Committee, a Nomination Committee, a Remuneration and Assessment Committee, and a Related-Party Transaction Committee, and has formulated corresponding working rules, clarifying the responsibilities, decision-making procedures, and rules of procedure of each special committee. Since its establishment, the Company's shareholders' meeting, board of directors, and supervisory board/audit committee have operated independently and effectively in accordance with the Company Law and other relevant laws and regulations, with no violations of laws or regulations.

(I) Shareholders' Meeting System

The Company has formulated the Rules of Procedure for Shareholders' Meetings. From the establishment of the joint stock limited company to the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Company has held 11 shareholders' meetings in total. The shareholders' meetings have operated in a regulated manner in accordance with laws and regulations including the Company Law and the Securities Law, the Articles of Association, and the Rules of Procedure for Shareholders' Meetings and other corporate rules. Shareholders attended the meetings in person, by correspondence, or by proxy. The shareholders' meetings have complied with the relevant laws, regulations, and corporate rules in terms of the manner of convening, procedures for deliberation, voting methods, and content of resolutions, and there are no circumstances of exercising authority in violation of relevant laws, regulations, or corporate rules.

(II) Board of Directors System

The Company has formulated the Rules of Procedure for the Board of Directors. From the establishment of the joint stock limited company to the date of signing of this Prospectus, the Company has held 24 board of directors meetings in total. The board of directors has operated in a regulated manner in accordance with laws and regulations including the Company Law and the Securities Law, the Articles of Association, and the Rules of Procedure for the Board of Directors and other corporate rules, and all directors attended each meeting. The board of directors has complied with the relevant laws, regulations, and corporate rules in terms of the manner of convening, procedures for deliberation, voting methods, and content of resolutions, and there are no circumstances of exercising authority in violation of relevant laws, regulations, or corporate rules. In addition, the Company has established an Executive Committee, which is the Company's operational management decision-making body under the leadership of the Board of Directors and is accountable to the Board of Directors.

(III) Audit Committee System

In accordance with the requirements of the Company Law, the Articles of Association, and relevant laws, regulations, and other normative documents, the Issuer has established an Audit Committee and formulated the Working Rules of the Board of Directors Audit Committee. In June 2025, the Issuer convened a shareholders' meeting to abolish the supervisory board and have the Audit Committee exercise the powers of the supervisory board as stipulated in the Company Law; detailed and explicit provisions were made regarding the responsibilities and authority of the Audit Committee, the decision-making procedures of the Audit Committee, the rules of procedure of the Audit Committee, and other matters, thereby establishing a relatively sound Audit Committee system.

The Audit Committee consists of 3 members elected by the Board of Directors, including 2 independent directors. The convener of the Audit Committee is an independent director who is an accounting professional.

Prior to the abolition of the supervisory board in June 2025, the Issuer, in accordance with the requirements of the Company Law, the Articles of Association, and relevant laws, regulations, and other normative documents, had made detailed and explicit provisions in the Articles of Association regarding the powers of the supervisory board, notices to the supervisory board, the convening of supervisory board meetings, and voting and resolutions of the supervisory board, thereby establishing a relatively sound supervisory board system. The Issuer's supervisory board consisted of 3 supervisors. During the Reporting Period, the Issuer held 4 supervisory board meetings in total. The supervisory board complied with the relevant laws, regulations, and corporate rules in terms of the manner of convening, procedures for deliberation, voting methods, and content of resolutions, and there were no circumstances of exercising authority in violation of relevant laws, regulations, or corporate rules.

(IV) Independent Director System

The Company's Board of Directors has 4 independent directors, representing at least one-third of the total number of directors, including 1 accounting professional. Since their appointment, the independent directors have diligently fulfilled their duties in accordance with laws and regulations including the Company Law and the Securities Law, the Articles of Association, and other corporate rules; they have attended all board of directors meetings on time, actively participated in the Company's decision-making, carefully deliberated on matters requiring the independent directors to express independent opinions, and issued independent opinions. The independent directors have played a positive role in improving the Company's governance structure and standardizing its operations. The extensive professional knowledge and diligent work ethics of the independent directors have played a commendable role in the Board of Directors' formulation of the Company's development strategy, development plans, and production and operation decisions, effectively ensuring the scientific rigor and impartiality of the Company's operational decisions.

(V) Board Secretary System

The Company has 1 Board Secretary, who is appointed or dismissed by the Board of Directors, and is primarily responsible for the preparation of the Company's shareholders' meetings and board of directors meetings, handling information disclosure matters, and other related affairs. The Board Secretary is a senior management member of the Company and is accountable to the Company and the Board of Directors. Since taking up the position, the Board Secretary has strictly prepared board of directors and shareholders' meetings in accordance with the relevant provisions of the Articles of Association and the Board Secretary Working System, and has diligently fulfilled the relevant duties.

VIII. Description of the Composition of the Audit Committee and Other Special Committees

The Issuer's Board of Directors has established the following special committees: the Audit Committee, the Strategy Committee, the Nomination Committee, the Remuneration and Assessment Committee, and the Related-Party Transaction Committee. The special committees of the Board of Directors serve as specialized working bodies of the Board of Directors; the special committees are accountable to the Board of Directors, and proposals from each special committee are submitted to the Board of Directors for deliberation and decision. The Issuer has formulated the Working Rules of the Board of Directors Audit Committee, the Working Rules of the Board of Directors Strategy Committee, the Working Rules of the Board of Directors Remuneration and Assessment Committee, the Working Rules of the Board of Directors Nomination Committee, and the Working Rules of the Related-Party Transaction Committee. XI. Specific Use of Proceeds Raised

(I) Memory Wafer Manufacturing Production Line Technology Upgrade and Renovation Project

1. Project Overview

This project proposes to carry out technical renovation at the Issuer's subsidiaries to progressively achieve a transition to mid-to-high-end product production line process technology, including technical renovation and upgrades to process steps such as etching, thin film deposition, and cleaning, thereby completing the renovation and upgrade of the process product line. Through the implementation of this project, on one hand, the technological renovation and upgrade of the DRAM production line can be achieved, reducing production costs. On the other hand, the technical upgrade and renovation can also be used to simultaneously introduce and establish cooperation with domestically produced and new types of equipment, materials, components, etc., promoting the localization and diversification of the industrial chain and supply chain, and enhancing the Company's risk resilience.

2. Project Investment Estimate

The total investment amount for this project is RMB 7.500 billion. The specific investment estimate is as follows:

No. Item Investment Amount (RMB 100 million) Proportion of Total Investment
1 Construction and civil engineering costs 3.34 4.45%
2 Equipment procurement and installation costs 46.66 62.22%
3 Intangible assets 25.00 33.33%
Total 75.00 100.00%

3. Project Timeline and Schedule

The construction period of this project is 3 years. The preliminary design and construction design of the project are to be completed before the third quarter of 2025; the facility renovation, machine move-in, machine commissioning, and machine production launch are to be implemented in batches before the end of 2027; and the completion acceptance is to be concluded before the first half of 2028.

4. Project Site Selection

This project is planned to be constructed and implemented within the existing plant area of the Issuer's subsidiaries, and does not involve the purchase of new land.

(II) DRAM Memory Technology Upgrade Project

1. Project Overview

This project proposes to carry out technical renovation on the DRAM memory demonstration line project, including technical renovation and upgrades to process steps such as etching, thin film deposition, and cleaning. The core upgrade content includes process improvements and product iterations. Through the implementation of this project, the Issuer's subsidiaries will be elevated to a newer-generation process technology platform.

2. Project Investment Estimate

3. Project Timeline and Schedule

The construction period of this project is 3 years. Before the third quarter of 2025, the preliminary work for the project is to be completed, including the preliminary design, capacity planning, and equipment demand planning. Starting from the fourth quarter of 2025, the equipment procurement plan is to be initiated; equipment move-in and commissioning are planned to be implemented in batches before the end of 2026 to 2027; and the completion acceptance is to be concluded before the first half of 2028.

(III) Dynamic Random Access Memory (DRAM) Forward-Looking Technology Research and Development Project

1. Project Overview

This project focuses on forward-looking technology research required for future advanced DRAM. The Company will, based on market and customer demand trends and the future frontier technology development direction of DRAM, carry out research and development work on forward-looking technologies compatible with DRAM. The Issuer plans to invest RMB 9.0 billion of the raised funds to carry out forward-looking technology research and development work, expand the Company's independent innovation capabilities and R&D level in related fields, and maintain the Company's continuous technological leadership.

2. Project Investment Estimate

The total investment amount for this project is RMB 9.000 billion, primarily including R&D personnel costs, fixed asset investments, materials and spare parts and other expenses, and testing and services.
